feature_claims.text: The Stellar is a single-lumen, flexible composite guide catheter with variable stiffness characteristics. Manufactured by Balt USA LLC and marketed under FDA clearance, the Stellar provides reliable access and support for neurovascular procedures with a lubricious hydrophilic-coated surface and radiopaque marker band for fluoroscopic visualization.
- Variable stiffness design enables trackability in complex anatomy
- Proven in neurovascular access applications

The Revere is a hybrid access catheter combining coil-wound flexibility with braided shaft pushability to optimize trackability and support during neurovascular procedures. Designed for use as an access and support catheter in stroke intervention and other neurovascular cases, the Revere enables reliable device delivery through complex vascular anatomy.
- Hydrophilic coating enables smooth navigation
- Flexible yet pushable design enables distal access
- Ultra-low profile enables navigation to distal targets
- Proven effectiveness in AVM embolization
=== CLINICAL EVIDENCE ===

data.Press Release Title: Balt USA Announces CE Mark Obtained for Titan Aspiration Catheter
data.Press Release Content: Balt USA has obtained CE mark approval for the Titan aspiration catheter for neurovascular stroke treatment. The Titan catheter line comprises two configurations: large bore catheter (0.070") and smaller bore catheter (0.036") for navigation and support. Announced May 14, 2019. CEO Pascal Girin stated: 'Titan marks the sixth significant product approval obtained by Balt within the last year and contributes to Balt's rapid succession of products to the neurovascular community.' Note: Not cleared or approved for sale in the United States.

data.Press Release Title: Raptor Aspiration Catheter Receives FDA 510(k) Clearance (K234083)
data.Press Release Content: Raptor Aspiration Catheter FDA 510(k) Clearance K234083 - Balt device. The Raptor is a single-lumen, coil reinforced, variable stiffness composite catheter that facilitates removal of thrombus from the neurovasculature when connected to a compatible aspiration pump. The catheter features 60cm of hydrophilic coating to facilitate smooth navigation, a 20cm distal segment with coil design for improved navigability, and is available in 0.071" and 0.074" inner diameter sizes. Indicated for revascularization of patients with acute ischemic stroke secondary to intracranial large vessel occlusive disease (internal carotid, middle cerebral M1 and M2 segments, basilar, and vertebral arteries) within 8 hours of symptom onset. The catheter has a distal radiopaque marker providing visual confirmation of the distal tip location under fluoroscopy. Safety and performance evaluated in comparative animal study in porcine model with assessments including performance, handling, aspiration of experimental soft and firm clots, and maximum vacuum application. Sources: https://baltgroup.com/products/raptor/, https://www.accessdata.fda.gov/scripts/cdrh/cfdocs/cfPMN/pmn.cfm?ID=K234083
data.Related Articles: [1] Venegas et al.. "Initial multicenter experience with the Raptor aspiration catheter for treating large vessel occlusi". Interventional Neuroradiology (2025). PMID: 41289167
data.Article Abstracts & Results:
--- [1] Initial multicenter experience with the Raptor aspiration catheter for treating  (Interventional Neuroradiology, 2025) ---
Background: Mechanical thrombectomy is standard care for ischemic stroke from large vessel occlusion. Direct aspiration techniques have shown comparable effectiveness to stent retrievers. Objective: To evaluate the safety and efficacy of the Raptor catheter as a first-line aspiration device for large vessel occlusion treatment in a multicenter setting. Methods: The study prospectively enrolled 46 patients with acute ischemic stroke due to large vessel occlusion. All patients received Raptor catheter treatment using direct aspiration technique. Researchers analyzed demographic, clinical, and procedural variables alongside efficacy and safety outcomes using descriptive statistics. Results: Median patient age was 66 years (39.1% women). The National Institutes of Health Stroke Scale score at admission was 18. The M1 segment accounted for 67.4% of occlusions. First-pass effect was achieved in 54.3% (25/46), and final recanalization in 93.5% (43/46). Median passes required was 1, with mean puncture-to-recanalization time of approximately 26 minutes. Rescue stent retrievers were needed in 17.4% of cases. Complications occurred in 10.9% of patients, with symptomatic intracranial hemorrhage in 6.5%. Ninety-day mortality was 6.5%, and 61.9% achieved functional independence. Conclusion: The Raptor catheter demonstrated safety and effectiveness as a first-line strategy with low complication rates, though additional comparative studies are warranted.

data.Press Release Title: FDA Clears Balt's Optima Coil System for Neurovascular Procedures
data.Press Release Content: Balt USA announced 510(k) clearance from the FDA for its Optima coil system on March 20, 2018. The Optima system comprises three components: a coil, pusher, and instantaneous thermal detachment mechanism for treating strokes, aneurysms, and arteriovenous malformations. Over 60 successful clinical cases completed during Europe's limited market release. This was the first Balt USA product approved for U.S. market. Dr. Satoshi Tateshima stated: 'The Optima coil system provides physicians with an extremely soft coiling system and a rapid and reliable detachment system.'

data.Press Release Title: Balt Secures EUR 355 Million to Refinance Debt and Accelerate Growth
data.Press Release Content: Balt secured EUR 355 million on December 20, 2024, for refinancing debt and accelerating growth. Additional milestones: October 2025 reached 1,000 team members globally; June 2025 acquired Canadian distributor Yocan Medical Systems; November 2024 STEM trial results published in New England Journal of Medicine.

data.Press Release Title: Balt's Squid Liquid Embolic Receives FDA PMA Approval for Adjunctive Treatment of Chronic Subdural Hematomas (STEM Trial, Published NEJM)
data.Press Release Content: Balt Squid Liquid Embolic FDA PMA Approval STEM Trial - Balt, Inc. received FDA Premarket Approval (PMA) for Squid liquid embolic agent on February 4, 2026. Indicated for embolization of the middle meningeal artery (MMA) as an adjunct to usual care treatment in patients with symptomatic chronic subdural hematoma(s) measuring 10 mm or greater in thickness. The STEM trial (Squid Trial for the Embolization of the Middle Meningeal Artery) was the first prospective, randomized study assessing the impact of embolization on treatment failures in this patient population. STEM was a prospective, multi-center, international randomized controlled trial enrolling 310 patients comparing those who received embolization of the MMA with Squid Liquid Embolic as an adjunct to standard surgical or medical management against those who did not. Published in New England Journal of Medicine in November 2024, the study demonstrated that adjunctive embolization significantly reduced treatment failure rates without increasing adverse events. Primary outcome: 19 of 120 patients (16%) in the embolization group versus 47 of 129 patients (36%) in the control group, corresponding to a 64% relative reduction in risk. Squid is an ethyl vinyl alcohol (EVOH) copolymer-based device available in three viscosities (12, 18, and 34) with micronized tantalum. Originally approved in Europe in 2012. Pascal Girin, Balt CEO, noted this represents the company's first PMA approval and a major milestone. Sources: https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2026/02/04/3232016/0/en/Balt-s-Squid-Liquid-Embolic-Receives-FDA-Approval-for-Adjunctive-Treatment-for-Patients-with-Chronic-Subdural-Hematomas.html, https://baltgroup.com/news/stem-trial-results-in-the-new-england-journal-of-medicine-nejm/, https://www.neurologylive.com/view/bal-squid-receives-premarket-approval-patients-chronic-subdural-hematomas

data.Press Release Title: Initial Multicenter Experience with the Raptor Aspiration Catheter for Treating LVO Ischemic Stroke
data.Press Release Content: Initial multicenter prospective study of Raptor aspiration catheter for acute ischemic stroke with large vessel occlusion. Study of 46 patients (median age 66, 39.1% women) with 67.4% M1 occlusions. First-pass recanalization (mTICI ≥2c): 54.3%. Final recanalization (mTICI ≥2b): 93.5%. Median passes: 1 (range 1-2). Puncture-to-recanalization: 25.7 ± 28.4 minutes. Symptomatic intracranial hemorrhage: 6.5%. 90-day mortality: 6.5%. Functional independence at 90 days: 61.9%. Rescue stent retrievers needed: 17.4%. Published November 25, 2025.

data.Press Release Title: Route 92 Medical Files Patent Infringement Suit Against Balt for Tenzing Delivery Technology
data.Press Release Content: Real World Data Supports Results of Route 92 Medical's SUMMIT MAX Clinical Trial. Route 92 Medical announced results from an independent real-world study of their HiPoint Reperfusion System, presented at the Society of NeuroInterventional Surgery annual meeting in July 2025. The retrospective multicenter study analyzed 193 consecutive patients treated with the HiPoint system who were not enrolled in the SUMMIT MAX trial. Clinicians successfully delivered the HiPoint Reperfusion System to the stroke-causing occlusion in 96.2% of cases. First-Pass Effect (mTICI 2c/3) was achieved in 57.5% of cases, and First-Pass Effect (mTICI 2b/2c/3) was achieved in 68.4% of cases. The median number of passes required was 1. Dr. Daniel Tonetti noted the results 'demonstrate strong delivery and first pass effect data, as well as low complication rates,' consistent with findings from the SUMMIT MAX trial. Route 92 Medical also introduced SUMMIT RISE, a prospective 500-patient study designed to evaluate their neurovascular interventional solutions across diverse clinical cases with independent core-lab adjudication.

data.Article Abstracts & Results:
--- [1] Improved catheter delivery for aspiration thrombectomy using Tenzing 7 ledge red (Interventional Neuroradiology, 2023) ---
Esperance aspiration catheter initial experience (Wallaby/phenox) - No results found in PubMed database.

--- [2] Novel Tenzing 7 Delivery Catheter for Thrombectomy in Acute Stroke: A Clinical M (Stroke: Vascular and Interventional Neurology, 2024) ---
Penumbra ADAPT FAST study - Found 'Vessel diameter and catheter-to-vessel ratio affect the success rate of clot aspiration' (PMID: 32753556) which directly addresses the ADAPT technique. Study states: 'A direct aspiration first pass technique (ADAPT) is an efficient, safe, cost-effective, and fast thrombectomy technique.' Evaluated 'anatomical and clot characteristics associated with success of the aspiration component.' Full abstract and results sections not available in search results page.

--- [3] Tenzing 7 Delivery Catheter Performance Across Tortuosity Indices (Stroke: Vascular and Interventional Neurology, 2024) ---
Scepter XC balloon catheter clinical use (MicroVention) - Found 3 studies mentioning Scepter XC: (1) Proximal Protection Technique for Migrated Closed-Cell Stents (2025, PMID: 40062123) - device navigated 'through the stent's cells to occlude the external carotid artery' during complication management; (2) Endovascular Treatment of Distal Lenticulostriate Artery Aneurysm (2020, PMID: 31954888) - 4×11mm Scepter XC balloon catheter utilized for selective artery catheterization with balloon-blocking technique; (3) Combined Transradial and Transfemoral Approach (2021, PMID: 33484261) - device employed for emboli protection during vertebral artery intervention in subclavian stenosis stenting. Full abstracts and results sections not available.

--- [4] Tenzing Assisted Delivery of Aspiration (TADA) technique for thrombectomy of med (Journal of Neurointerventional Surgery, 2024) ---
Neuroform Atlas HDE cohort - Prospective, multicenter clinical trial evaluated Neuroform Atlas Stent System for treating unruptured wide-necked intracranial aneurysms. RESULTS: Patient Population: 30 patients enrolled across 8 U.S. centers, 27 completed 12-month angiographic follow-up, Mean age 59.4 years; 80% female, Mean aneurysm size 5.3 mm. Primary Efficacy Endpoint: 'The primary efficacy endpoint was observed in 26/30 patients (86.7%, 95% CI 69.3% to 96.2%)' achieving complete aneurysm occlusion without retreatment or significant vessel narrowing. Safety Outcomes: Procedural success rate: 100% for stent deployment, 'The primary safety endpoint of stroke occurred in one patient (3.3%), who made a complete clinical recovery' at discharge, Zero neurological deaths reported. Conclusion: 'the Neuroform Atlas stent in conjunction with coils' delivered high angiographic occlusion rates with improved safety profile.

--- [5] Tenzing 7 delivery catheter with or without a leading microwire for single pass  (Journal of Neurointerventional Surgery, 2024) ---
Introduction: Aspiration mechanical thrombectomy traditionally includes use of an inner microcatheter and leading microwire to navigate an aspiration catheter (AC) to the site of occlusion. The study examined whether the Tenzing 7 catheter could effectively deliver treatment without this standard microwire support. Methods: Researchers retrospectively analyzed consecutive patients undergoing single-pass thrombectomy with the Tenzing 7 device across six institutions between 2020-2022. They measured successful catheter delivery rates, time from puncture to revascularization, and procedural complications. Results: Among 89 patients, a leading microwire was used in 21% of cases and omitted in 79%. Successful AC delivery was similar with and without microwires (97% vs. 90%, p = 0.15). Median treatment times were comparable between groups (17 minutes with microwire versus 16 minutes without, p = 0.12). Complication rates showed no significant differences between approaches. Conclusion: The findings suggest that leading microwire use was infrequently necessary with the T7 delivery catheter. The device achieved equivalent success and safety outcomes whether or not a microwire was employed, potentially allowing operators to reserve microwires for challenging anatomical cases.

data.Press Release Title: Stryker Receives FDA PMA Approval for Surpass Streamline Flow Diverter to Treat Large/Giant Unruptured Aneurysms
data.Press Release Content: Stryker Receives FDA Approval for Surpass Streamline Flow Diverter. Stryker Corporation announced that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration granted pre-market approval (PMA) for the Surpass Streamline Flow Diverter to treat unruptured large and giant wide-neck intracranial aneurysms on July 13, 2018 (PMA Number P170024). The Surpass Streamline Flow Diverter is a small cobalt chromium braided stent that directs blood flow within an intracranial artery away from a weakened blood vessel sac or aneurysm. The device is the second flow diverting stent to gain FDA approval in the U.S. It is indicated for use in endovascular treatment of patients 18 years or older with unruptured large or giant saccular wide-neck (neck width ≥4 mm or dome-to-neck ratio <2) or fusiform intracranial aneurysms in the internal carotid artery from the petrous segment to the terminus. The SCENT Investigational Device Exemption trial provided clinical support showing 62.8% (113/180) of patients had complete intracranial aneurysm occlusion (Raymond-Roy Class I) without clinically significant in-stent stenosis or retreatment within 12 months post-procedure. An estimated 6 million Americans have unruptured brain aneurysms, with approximately 25,000 treated annually. Mark Paul, president of Stryker's Neurovascular division, emphasized this approval expands commercial footprint into the flow diversion market.

data.Press Release Title: Stryker Launches Surpass Elite Flow Diverter in the U.S., South Korea and Europe — Features BioStealth Surface Technology
data.Press Release Content: Stryker announced commercial launch of Surpass Elite Flow Diverter in U.S., South Korea, and Europe as of July 22, 2025. Device: Next-generation flow diverter with BioStealth Surface (proprietary neutralization process creating more thromboresistant cobalt chromium material, supported by in vitro and in vivo studies) and enhanced implant design (higher braid angle with optimized heat treatment enabling better opening and improved vessel-wall apposition). Regulatory: FDA approval and CE Mark received in 2024. Key Quote: Dr. Alejandro Tomasello stated Surpass Elite delivers outstanding performance and exceptional ease of use.

data.Device/Product: Galaxy G3 Mini Embolic Coil + EmboTrap Data
data.Press Release Title: Cerenovus Launches Galaxy G3 Mini Embolic Coil; Touts ARISE II EmboTrap Study Data
data.Press Release Content: Cerenovus Galaxy G3 Mini Embolic Coil ARISE II EmboTrap data - Johnson & Johnson's Cerenovus division announced the launch of its Galaxy G3 Mini embolic coil and released data from the ARISE II study of its Embotrap device. The Galaxy G3 Mini coils are approximately 25% softer than Galaxy G3 xsft coils with an ultra-low coil profile and primary wind diameter of 0.009 inches, the lowest profile available in the Spectra line. The coils feature a random complex shape for conformability, enabling the device to seek and fill open spaces in cerebral aneurysms for higher packing densities, shown to lower patient retreatment rates. Cerenovus received 510(k) clearance for the Embotrap device from the FDA in late September 2017. ARISE II study results showed high success rates in restoring blood flow with high rates of functional independence in patients treated with the Embotrap device. The Galaxy G3 Mini launch and ARISE II data presentation occurred in January 2018. Sources: https://www.massdevice.com/jjs-cerenovus-launches-galaxy-g3-mini-embolic-coil-touts-arise-ii-embotrap-study-data/, https://neuronewsinternational.com/cerenovus-launches-next-generation-embolic-coil/, https://cardiacvascularnews.com/galaxy-g3-mini-coil-is-the-smallest-and-softest-finishing-coil-the-company-has-ever-produced/

data.Press Release Title: Early Postmarket Results with EmboTrap II Stent Retriever for Mechanical Thrombectomy: A Multicenter Experience. Published in AJNR.
data.Press Release Content: EmboTrap II Early Postmarket Results Mechanical Thrombectomy AJNR 2021 - Published in American Journal of Neuroradiology (AJNR), May 2021, Volume 42, Issue 5, Pages 904-909. Title: 'Early Postmarket Results with EmboTrap II Stent Retriever for Mechanical Thrombectomy: A Multicenter Experience'. The EmboTrap II is a novel stent retriever with a dual-layer design and distal mesh. Study included seventy patients (mean age 69.9 years; 48.6% women) with emergent large-vessel occlusions in the ICA (n=18), M1 (n=38), M2 or M3 (n=13), and basilar artery (n=1). Key results: TICI 2b recanalization achieved in 95.7% overall (82.3% in EmboTrap II-only cases), first-pass efficacy achieved in 35.7%. Definitive treatment with alternative device required in 12.9% of cases. Hemorrhagic conversion noted in 22.9% (4.3% symptomatic). Conclusion: Initial postmarket results with EmboTrap II are favorable and comparable with other commercially available stent retrievers. This represents the first postmarket prospective multicenter experience following FDA approval. Sources: https://www.ajnr.org/content/42/5/904, https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33707283/, https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8115375/

data.Press Release Title: MASTRO I: Meta-Analysis Comparing EmboTrap, Solitaire and Trevo — EmboTrap Had Significantly Better Rates of Good Neurological Function
data.Press Release Content: MASTRO I meta-analysis compared EmboTrap against Trevo and Solitaire stent retrievers. Published May 10, 2023. Key findings: EmboTrap achieved 57.4% for 90-day mRS 0-2 outcomes versus 50% for Trevo (p=0.013) and 45.3% for Solitaire (p<0.001). Symptomatic intracranial hemorrhage: EmboTrap 3.9% versus 7.75% for Solitaire (p=0.028). Device features: closed distal end for clot control, multi-segmented design, inner channel functioning as temporary bypass and clot retention mechanism. Featured expert: Waleed Brinjikji (Mayo Clinic).

data.Press Release Title: Johnson & Johnson's Cerenovus Launches New Clot Removal Devices to Treat Stroke (CereGlide 92)
data.Press Release Content: Cerenovus CereGlide 92 new stroke clot removal devices Johnson Johnson - Johnson & Johnson MedTech announced the launch of the CEREGLIDE 92 Catheter System on February 12, 2025, a next-generation .092" catheter with the INNERGLIDE 9 delivery aid. CEREGLIDE 92 is a .092" inner diameter catheter system that allows physicians to achieve large distal access and provides flow reduction in the M1 when inserting devices for revascularization of acute ischemic stroke patients. Features include: co-packaged INNERGLIDE 9 Delivery Aid, full catheter visibility with BRITE-LINE Technology, TruCourse Technology (increases distal tip flexibility aiding trackability). By utilizing CEREGLIDE 92 to insert aspiration catheters during thrombectomy, physicians can restore blood flow by directly withdrawing a blood clot or inserting a catheter in combination with a stent retriever. Larger lumen provides distal support for multiple neurovascular tools in aneurysm and hemorrhagic complication treatment. Now commercially available in the U.S. with phased rollout planned across select European markets in autumn. Sources: https://www.jnjmedtech.com/en-US/news-events/johnson-johnson-medtech-launches-cereglide-92-catheter-system-acute-ischemic-stroke, https://www.jnj.com/media-center/press-releases/johnson-johnson-medtech-launches-cereglide-92-catheter-system-for-acute-ischemic-stroke

data.Press Release Title: EXCELLENT Registry: Prospective Multicenter Global Registry of Endovascular Stroke Treatment with EmboTrap — 94.5% Final mTICI ≥2b. Published in Stroke.
data.Press Release Content: EXCELLENT Registry EmboTrap endovascular stroke treatment Stroke journal 2024 - The EXCELLENT Registry is a prospective, multicenter, global registry of patients with acute ischemic stroke treated with EMBOTRAP as first-line mechanical thrombectomy device. Conducted at 34 sites (25 sites contributing clot) from September 2018 to March 2021. Registry utilized core imaging and central histology laboratories blinded to clinical data, independent 90-day modified Rankin Scale assessment, and Clinical Events Committee review. After screening 3799 patients, 997 subjects were included (mean age 70.0 ± 14.2 years; 51.8% women; 19.7% non-White). Key results: First-pass mTICI ≥2b rate was 64.5% (623/966), first-pass mTICI ≥2c was 39.1% (378/966), final mTICI ≥2b was 94.5% (931/985). Good clinical outcomes (mRS 0-2 or no worsening from baseline) noted in 46.8%, average NIHSS decrease 6.4 ± 7.9 points. Symptomatic intracranial hemorrhage rate 1.6%, all-cause mortality 19.1%. Published in Stroke journal in 2024. Sources: https://www.ahajournals.org/doi/10.1161/STROKEAHA.124.047324, https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39559856/, https://evtoday.com/news/cerenovus-embotrap-devices-evaluated-in-excellent-registry-of-mechanical-thrombectomy-for-acute-ischemic-stroke

data.Press Release Title: Johnson & Johnson MedTech Launches New CereGlide Aspiration Catheters (42 and 57) in US
data.Press Release Content: CereGlide Aspiration Catheters 42 57 Johnson Johnson MedTech launch - Johnson & Johnson MedTech launched the CEREGLIDE 42 and CEREGLIDE 57 aspiration catheters along with INNERGLIDE 7 delivery aid as the latest additions to J&J MedTech's aspiration-first stroke portfolio. Both catheters feature proprietary variable-stiffness construction providing flexible distal navigation and supportive proximal control. Design features include hydrophilic coating for reduced friction in tortuous anatomy, radiopaque tip marker for fluoroscopic visibility, and TruCourse Technology for easier M2 tracking. Designed to help physicians reach and remove distally located clots more consistently while simplifying procedural decisions and supporting performance across broad range of ischemic stroke cases. The CEREGLIDE family now includes 42, 57, and 71 aspiration catheters providing range of devices for performance, consistency, and compatibility. Sources: https://www.jnj.com/media-center/press-releases/johnson-johnson-completes-catheter-portfolio-with-launch-of-cereglide-42-cereglide-57-and-innerglide-7, https://www.massdevice.com/johnson-johnson-medtech-launches-new-cereglide-catheters/, https://neuronewsinternational.com/jj-adds-cereglide-42-cereglide-57-and-innerglide-7-to-stroke-catheter-portfolio/

data.Press Release Title: Early Results with EmboTrap III Stent Retriever: Multicenter US Experience — Favorable Efficacy and Safety Comparable to Other Stent Retrievers
data.Press Release Content: Multicenter US retrospective study of EmboTrap III stent retriever across 4 academic centers, December 2022-February 2023. 92 patients with acute ischemic stroke from large vessel occlusion (median age 72, 56.5% women, mean NIHSS 16.6 ± 8.1). EmboTrap III features: longer lengths (5 mm × 22 mm, 5 mm × 37 mm, 6.5 mm × 45 mm), denser distal mesh design, enhanced radiopacity. Results: 91.3% successful final reperfusion (mTICI ≥ 2b), 53.3% first-pass effect with complete reperfusion, 79.3% modified first-pass effect, 22.9% functional independence at 90 days (mRS ≤ 2), no device-related complications, symptomatic hemorrhagic transformation 5.4%.

data.Press Release Title: J&J's Codman Neuro Acquires Irish Stroke-Care Company Neuravi (EmboTrap Developer) for Undisclosed Price
data.Press Release Content: Codman Neuro acquires Neuravi EmboTrap stroke company - Codman Neuro announced the acquisition of Neuravi Limited on April 10, 2017. Neuravi is a privately held Ireland-based company. Financial terms not disclosed. DePuy Ireland Unlimited Company, an affiliate of Codman Neuro, was the acquiring entity. Neuravi produces the EmboTrap and EmboTrap II revascularization platforms designed to capture clots and restore blood flow in stroke patients. Founded in 2009, Neuravi had invested extensively in scientific research on clot varieties causing acute ischemic stroke and translated findings into EmboTrap Revascularization Platform. EmboTrap and EmboTrap II were commercially available in Europe and available in U.S. for investigational use only under ARISE II clinical trial, with FDA submission planned for later in 2017. Acquisition demonstrated Codman Neuro's strong commitment to delivering innovative stroke therapy products. This acquisition, along with Codman Neuro's acquisition of Pulsar Vascular, led to formation of J&J's Cerenovus neurovascular business in 2017. Sources: https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/codman-neuro-announces-acquisition-of-neuravi-limited-to-accelerate-innovation-in-acute-ischemic-stroke-therapy-300436838.html, https://www.massdevice.com/codman-neuro-acquires-neuravi/, https://www.fiercebiotech.com/medical-devices/j-j-s-codman-neuro-snaps-up-strokecare-company-neuravi-for-undisclosed-price

data.Press Release Title: Reports: Johnson & Johnson Exploring Sale of Cerenovus Stroke Business for $1B+
data.Press Release Content: Johnson Johnson exploring sale Cerenovus stroke business - Johnson & Johnson is exploring the sale of its Cerenovus neurovascular stroke therapy business, according to reports from February 2025. The asking price is believed to be more than $1 billion, with early discussions held with potential buyers. J&J formed Cerenovus in 2017 by combining acquisitions of Pulsar Vascular and Neuravi with remainder of Codman Neuro neurological portfolio following $1 billion divestiture. Cerenovus headquartered in Irvine, California and operates as a leading neurovascular interventions company. The potential sale aligns with broader industry trend where companies shift toward high-growth, high-margin markets. J&J's recent major cardiovascular acquisitions (Abiomed $16.6bn, Shockwave Medical $13.1bn, V-Wave $1.7bn) demonstrate commitment to cardiovascular interventions sector, expected to outpace neurovascular market growth. Financial Times report emphasized that nothing may come of early discussions; if J&J does not receive expected offers, company could postpone sale until market changes. Sources: https://www.massdevice.com/johnson-johnson-medtech-cerenovus-stroke-business-for-sale/, https://finance.yahoo.com/news/johnson-johnson-explores-sale-stroke-121157548.html, https://www.medicaldevice-network.com/analyst-comment/jj-potential-neurovascular-exit/

data.Press Release Title: J&J MedTech's Cerenovus Launches Next-Gen CereGlide 71 Intermediate Catheter with TruCourse for AIS Revascularization
data.Press Release Content: Cerenovus Launches Cereglide 71 Intermediate Catheter for Revascularization in Acute Ischemic Stroke. Cerenovus, a Johnson & Johnson MedTech division, introduced the Cereglide 71 intermediate catheter designed to treat acute ischemic stroke patients through revascularization procedures. Date: February 7, 2024. The device incorporates TruCourse Technology to enhance flexibility, enabling physicians to navigate challenging anatomical conditions and access blood clots more effectively. It functions as both a direct aspiration tool and a delivery mechanism for compatible stent retrievers like the Embotrap III. Dr. Fawaz Al-Mufti, Director of Neuroendovascular Surgery Fellowship at Westchester Medical Center, praised the device: 'CEREGLIDE 71...navigates, accesses occlusion sites, and engages with effectiveness to aspirate clots, rapidly restoring blood flow.' The device is part of Cerenovus' expanding Cereglide catheter family within their Stroke Solutions portfolio. It will be included in the Cerenovus EXCELLENT Registry, a real-world study on mechanical thrombectomy outcomes. Electronic instructions for use reflect the company's sustainability initiatives aimed at reducing environmental impact. The catheter is currently available in the United States.

data.Press Release Title: ARISE II Results Presented at ISC 2018: 80% Revascularization Within 3 Passes, 67% Functionally Independent at 90 Days. Published in Stroke.
data.Press Release Content: ARISE II trial data presented at International Stroke Conference (ISC 2018) on January 24-26, 2018 in Los Angeles by Dr. Osama Zaidat. Device: Embotrap stent retriever acquired by Cerenovus through purchase of Neuravi Limited in April 2017. 228 patients with large vessel blockages and severe neurological deficits within 8 hours of symptom onset. Results: 80% restoration of blood flow within three attempts, 93% vessels successfully opened by procedure end, 50% substantial reperfusion on first attempt, 67% functionally independent at 90-day follow-up. Dr. Zaidat noted 'very encouraging outcomes' regarding revascularization speed and success rates.

data.Press Release Title: Johnson & Johnson Introduces New Cerenovus Neurovascular Business — Combining Neuravi, Pulsar Vascular, and Codman Neuro Assets
data.Press Release Content: Johnson & Johnson announced Cerenovus neurovascular business on July 24, 2017 at SNIS 14th annual meeting in Colorado Springs. Cerenovus operates as part of DePuy Synthes Products under J&J Medical Devices Companies segment. Focus: delivering therapies for hemorrhagic and ischemic stroke patients. Combines newly acquired Pulsar Vascular Inc. (endovascular treatment of wide-neck bifurcation aneurysms) and Neuravi (EmboTrap revascularization platform for acute ischemic stroke).

data.Press Release Title: Cerenovus Expands Minimally Invasive Stroke Care Innovation with New TRUFILL n-BCA Liquid Embolic System Procedural Set
data.Press Release Content: Cerenovus announced launch of TRUFILL n-BCA Liquid Embolic System Procedural Set on March 28, 2024. FDA approved September 29, 2023. Designed for minimally invasive treatment of hemorrhagic stroke, specifically pre-surgical devascularization of cerebral arteriovenous malformations (AVMs). Bundled set includes 1mL and 3mL syringes, glass beaker, 18G blunt fill needle, 21G hypodermic needles, syringe labels. Mark Dickinson (Worldwide President of CERENOVUS): 'The procedural set was designed to address real-world challenges in the treatment of hemorrhagic stroke – simplifying procedure preparation.' Dr. Johanna Fifi (Mount Sinai): set 'eliminated the guesswork and sourcing for materials' needed for procedure prep.

data.Press Release Title: Cerenovus Receives FDA 510(k) Clearance for EmboTrap II Revascularization Device — Next-Gen Stent Retriever for Ischemic Stroke
data.Press Release Content: Cerenovus received FDA 510(k) clearance on May 21, 2018 for EmboTrap II Revascularization Device for ischemic stroke treatment. Device works by gripping and retrieving clots within brain blood vessels with minimal compression. Indicated within eight hours of stroke symptom onset. Already approved for use in Europe with over 3,000 patients treated. ARISE II study (228 patients): 80% blood flow restoration within three passes, ~50% restoration in single pass, two-thirds functionally independent at 90-day follow-up. For patients ineligible for or who failed intravenous tissue plasminogen activator (IV t-PA) therapy.

data.Press Release Title: Real-World Outcomes Associated with EmboTrap Revascularization Device for Ischemic Stroke in the United States
data.Press Release Content: Retrospective database analysis using Premier Healthcare Database of 318 adult patients (mean age 68.5 years) who underwent mechanical thrombectomy for acute ischemic stroke using EmboTrap between July 2018 and December 2020. Device: EmboTrap (Cerenovus), dual layer design for thrombus retrieval. Results: ~25% discharged to home/home health, in-hospital mortality 10.7%, intracranial hemorrhage 16.7%, mean hospital length of stay 9.9 days, 30-day readmission rates 9.6% (all-cause), 5.9% (cardiovascular), 2.6% (stroke-related). Mean hospital costs: $47,367 per patient. First US study reporting real-world outcomes from retrospective database analysis for EmboTrap.

data.Press Release Title: Cerenovus Launches Global EXCELLENT Registry for EmboTrap II Revascularization Device
data.Press Release Content: Cerenovus launched EXCELLENT Registry on January 16, 2019 - described as 'the single largest global registry of its kind' for EmboTrap II Revascularization Device. Enrolling up to 1,000 ischemic stroke patients across ~50 clinical sites in US and Europe. Also received European CE Mark approval for Geometric Clot Extractor (GCE) device. Registry goals: determine how clot characteristics (size, composition, density) correlate with patient outcomes, comorbidities, and revascularization success rates. EmboTrap II features dual-layer design; FDA cleared 2018, available in Europe since 2016. GCE targets difficult cases (~20% of ischemic stroke cases).

data.Press Release Title: EmboTrap Receives CE Mark Approval in the European Union — First Commercialization
data.Press Release Content: Major acquisition: Codman Neuro acquired Neuravi Limited in April 2017 to accelerate stroke therapy innovation. EmboTrap Revascularization Platform treated over 3,000 patients in Europe. Device available in US for investigational use only under ARISE II clinical trial. ARISE II enrollment completed February 2017: 228 patients across 19 sites in US and Europe. EmboTrap II received CE Mark approval September 2016 with enhancements: broader clot length ranges, 0.021 inch microcatheter delivery. European commercial launch October 2015. Series B financing €19 million (June 2015), Series A €5.2 million (July 2012).

data.Press Release Title: ARISE II Pivotal Trial Begins for Neuravi's EmboTrap Stent Retriever
data.Press Release Content: Neuravi announced ARISE II pivotal trial for EmboTrap stent retriever treating acute ischemic stroke. International study enrolling 210 patients across up to 25 sites in Europe and US. Device features: dual-layer, stent-like structure with integrated distal protection zone preventing clot fragments from escaping during removal. European CE Mark approval with commercial availability in Europe; US investigational only. Study data to support future FDA application. Principal investigators: Sam Zaidat, MD (US) and Prof. Tommy Andersson, MD (Europe). First patient enrolled November 5, 2015 at Tennessee Interventional and Imaging Associates at Erlanger Medical Center, Chattanooga.

data.Press Release Title: Enrollment Completed in ARISE II Pivotal Trial for Neuravi's EmboTrap II — 228 Patients at 19 Sites
data.Press Release Content: Neuravi announced completion of ARISE II pivotal clinical trial enrollment on February 22, 2017 ahead of schedule. EmboTrap II revascularization device: 228 patients enrolled across 19 sites in US and Europe. Device designed to restore blood flow by retrieving and retaining clot using dual-layer stent structure. Received CE Mark approval September 2016, commercially available in Europe. Data to be submitted to FDA for market clearance. Principal Investigators: Sam Zaidat, MD (US) and Tommy Andersson, MD (Europe). Dr. Zaidat emphasized advancement in 'refinements of mechanical thrombectomy that will yield better patient outcomes.' Dr. Raul Nogueira noted importance of advanced tools for stroke treatment.

data.Press Release Title: Neuravi Wins CE Mark for EmboTrap II Stent Retriever — Second Generation with Dual-Layer Design
data.Press Release Content: Neuravi EmboTrap II European Launch. Neuravi received Conformité Européenne (CE) Mark approval in September 2016 for the EmboTrap II Revascularization Device, marking its launch in Europe. The EmboTrap II is a next-gen version of the company's EmboTrap, designed to treat a broader range of clots with sizes that are deliverable through a 0.021" microcatheter. The EmboTrap II leverages the EmboTrap platform design, engineered to trap and remove a variety of clot types and rapidly re-establish flow, with an integrated fragment protection zone. Key improvements over first generation include a double proximal marker to allow more precise stent placement and an increase of outer cages from 3 to 5 to potentially improve the ability of the device to capture clots during device retraction. The EmboTrap and EmboTrap II revascularization devices are available in Europe to treat patients with acute ischemic stroke. In early evaluations at six centers, the EmboTrap II 5x33 demonstrated strong results: 16 of 17 cases resulted in TICI 2b-3 flow. The device remains investigational in the United States under the ARISE II clinical trial, with FDA submission anticipated following trial completion.

data.Press Release Title: Imperative Care Launches Dual Aspiration Thrombectomy Catheter for Ischaemic Stroke Treatment
data.Press Release Content: Imperative Care unveiled Zoom DuoPort technology enabling continuous dual aspiration technique (CDAT) on two Zoom aspiration catheters from single vacuum source for acute ischemic stroke treatment. Announced May 8, 2025. Device features: first system enabling continuous dual aspiration for stroke thrombectomy, compatible with large-bore 0.088-inch catheters for access and aspiration, physicians maintain aspiration control without switching between pumps. Zoom system received FDA 510(k) clearance earlier in 2025. Clinical trial data: 19-minute median case time from groin puncture to mTICI ≥2b reperfusion - fastest reported among major prospective mechanical thrombectomy trials. Dr. Hakeem Shakir (Oklahoma University Health) achieved first-pass TICI 3 revascularization.

data.Press Release Title: Imperative Care Secures FDA 510(k) Clearance for the Zoom System, Including First Large-Bore .088" Catheter for Aspiration
data.Press Release Content: Imperative Care Receives FDA Clearance for Zoom System. Imperative Care announced on January 27, 2025, that it received FDA 510(k) clearance of the company's Zoom stroke thrombectomy system, which now includes large-bore .088" catheters for both access and aspiration—a first for the company. The clearance expands the Zoom portfolio to encompass catheter sizes from .035" to .088", enabling comprehensive stroke treatment options. Clinical data from the prospective Imperative Trial (211 patients across 26 U.S. institutions) demonstrated: Fastest median time of 19 minutes from groin puncture to reperfusion, compared to referenced trials; High efficacy with 84% achieving optimal reperfusion within 3 passes without rescue devices; Strong safety profile with 0.9% symptomatic intracranial hemorrhage rate and 0.5% serious vessel injury rate. The Zoom system features a patented TRX™ tip design providing enhanced clot engagement and smooth tracking through challenging vasculature. Time is the most critical factor in driving positive patient outcomes, with the system designed for rapid, effective procedures. Imperative Care, Inc. is based in Campbell, California.

data.Press Release Title: Imperative Care Launches Zoom 4S Catheter to Further Advance Zoom Stroke System
data.Press Release Content: Imperative Care Launches Zoom 4S Catheter for Stroke Treatment. Imperative Care announced the commercial launch of the Zoom 4S Catheter in late January 2026, a new aspiration thrombectomy device designed for ischemic stroke patients. The device was built for ease of use, empowering physicians with a device designed to navigate a broad range of patient anatomies. The Zoom Stroke System, now enhanced with Zoom 4S, is engineered to simplify stroke thrombectomy procedures by reducing the number of device exchanges. The Zoom 4S Catheter brings the benefits of the ADAPT 2.0 technique to more patients. The device advances the ADAPT 2.0 technique by bringing together three clinical benefits: an intracranial catheter positioned closer to blood clots; asymmetric tip design for enhanced clot engagement; and continuous dual aspiration capability across two catheters. According to Richard Leparmentier, Chief Technology Officer, the innovation focuses on reducing treatment times and streamlining stroke procedures through a single system setup. The asymmetric tip design reportedly provides 15% greater clot engagement area compared to traditional flat-tip alternatives. The Zoom 4S joins Imperative Care's broader stroke portfolio, which includes multiple catheter sizes (35, 45, 55, 71, and 7X models). The device is indicated for patients with acute ischemic stroke from large vessel occlusion within 8 hours of symptom onset.

data.Press Release Title: Imperative Care Presents Positive Imperative Trial Data — M2 Occlusions Using the Zoom System (SNIS 2025)
data.Press Release Content: Imperative Care Presents Positive Imperative Trial Data for M2 Occlusions. Imperative Care announced clinical trial data on its Zoom System for treating M2 vessel occlusions in stroke patients. The research was presented as a late-breaking abstract at the Society of NeuroInterventional Surgery's 2025 annual meeting in Nashville. The Imperative Trial sub-analysis involved 65 patients with M2 occlusions (median age 69, median NIHSS score of 13). Results demonstrated: An 88% core-lab adjudicated rate of final mTICI≥2b reperfusion; 90-Day outcomes showed 56% achieved modified Rankin Scale (mRS) 0-1 and 62% achieved mRS 0-2; Safety Profile: Symptomatic intracranial hemorrhage rate of 1.5%, mortality rate of 4.6%, and all-cause intracranial hemorrhage rate of 18.5%. Imperative Care will fund the first randomized study of aspiration thrombectomy in M2 occlusions, comparing the Zoom System plus standard care against standard care alone. Investigator Justin Mascitelli noted the importance of generating evidence specifically for aspiration approaches in this patient population. The Zoom System is designed as a complete stroke intervention platform from vascular access through clot removal.

data.Press Release Title: EXTEND-IA Trial Published in NEJM: Solitaire FR Improved Reperfusion, Recovery, and Functional Outcome vs Alteplase Alone
data.Press Release Content: EXTEND-IA Trial Published in NEJM 2015. The EXTEND-IA trial was published on February 11, 2015, in the New England Journal of Medicine. The trial tested the hypothesis that patients with anterior circulation ischemic stroke selected with evidence of vessel occlusion and salvageable tissue on perfusion imaging within 4.5 hours would have improved reperfusion and early neurologic improvement when treated with early endovascular thrombectomy using the Solitaire FR stent retriever after intravenous administration of alteplase, compared with alteplase alone. Study design: Patients receiving 0.9 mg of alteplase per kilogram of body weight less than 4.5 hours after stroke onset were randomly assigned to undergo endovascular thrombectomy with the Solitaire FR stent retriever or continue receiving alteplase alone. All patients had occlusion of the internal carotid or middle cerebral artery with salvageable brain tissue and ischemic core of less than 70 ml on CT perfusion imaging. Key results: Endovascular therapy, initiated at a median of 210 minutes after stroke onset, increased early neurologic improvement at 3 days (80% vs. 37%, P=0.002) and improved functional outcome at 90 days, with more patients achieving functional independence (71% vs. 40%; P=0.01). There were no significant differences in rates of death or symptomatic intracerebral hemorrhage. The trial was stopped early after 70 patients had undergone randomization (35 patients in each group). The release of the MR CLEAN trial results prompted the data and safety monitoring board to review the data, and the trial was stopped early because efficacy was clearly shown.

data.Article Abstracts & Results:
--- [1] Solitaire flow restoration device versus the Merci Retriever in patients with ac (The Lancet, 2012) ---
TREVO 2 Trial (PMID 22932714) - Trevo versus Merci retrievers for thrombectomy

ABSTRACT:
Background: Mechanical thrombectomy devices fail to achieve recanalization in 20-40% of large vessel occlusion strokes. This trial compared the newer Trevo Retriever against the FDA-cleared Merci Retriever.

Methods: This open-label randomized controlled trial enrolled 178 patients (ages 18-85) across 27 sites with angiographically confirmed large vessel occlusions and NIHSS scores of 8-29 within 8 hours of symptom onset. Patients were randomly assigned 1:1 to receive either Trevo or Merci thrombectomy. The primary efficacy endpoint was "thrombolysis in cerebral infarction (TICI) scores of 2 or greater reperfusion with the assigned device alone." Safety outcomes measured procedure-related adverse events.

RESULTS:
Efficacy: 86% of Trevo patients (76/88) achieved the primary endpoint versus 60% of Merci patients (54/90), representing a statistically significant superiority (odds ratio 4.22, 95% CI 1.92-9.69; p<0.0001)

Safety: Adverse events occurred in 15% of Trevo patients (13/88) compared to 23% of Merci patients (21/90), with no significant difference between groups (p=0.1826)

Interpretation: The authors concluded that Trevo Retriever should be preferred over Merci for treating patients with large vessel occlusion strokes who are ineligible for or unresponsive to intravenous thrombolysis.

--- [2] Systematic Evaluation of Patients Treated With Neurothrombectomy Devices for Acu (Stroke, 2018) ---
The STRATIS registry (Systematic Evaluation of Patients Treated With Neurothrombectomy Devices for Acute Ischemic Stroke) was designed to prospectively enroll patients treated in the United States with a Solitaire Revascularization Device and Mindframe Capture Low Profile Revascularization Device within 8 hours from symptom onset. A total of 984 patients treated at 55 sites were analyzed. The primary results were published in Stroke in October 2017. Mechanical thrombectomy with stent retrievers has become standard of care for treatment of acute ischemic stroke patients because of large vessel occlusion, demonstrating process timelines, technical, and functional outcomes comparable to large randomized trials in a real world cohort.

--- [3] Impact of Balloon Guide Catheter Use on Clinical and Angiographic Outcomes in th (Stroke, 2019) ---
PMID 30776994 - STRATIS BGC (Impact of Balloon Guide Catheter Use)

ABSTRACT/RESULTS:
This multicenter study examined how balloon guide catheters (BGC) affected outcomes in acute ischemic stroke patients undergoing mechanical thrombectomy. The research analyzed 745 patients across three catheter types.

KEY FINDINGS:
First-pass recanalization: 48% with BGC versus 26% with conventional catheters
Functional independence at 3 months: 61% achieved good outcomes with BGC compared to 42% with conventional approach
Modified first-pass effect: 66% success rate for BGC versus 42% for standard catheters

Clinical Significance: According to the abstract, "BGC use was an independent predictor of FPE, modified FPE, and functional independence," suggesting routine adoption may enhance early revascularization success and patient recovery rates.

Study Design: STRATIS was a prospective, multicenter registry with independent core laboratory review blinded to clinical outcomes. It specifically evaluated the Solitaire stent retriever as first-line therapy for large vessel occlusions.

--- [4] Impact of Stent Retriever Size on Clinical and Angiographic Outcomes in the STRA (Stroke, 2019) ---
PMID 30626287 - STRATIS Size (Impact of Stent Retriever Size)

ABSTRACT:
This retrospective analysis examined how stent retriever size affects outcomes in acute ischemic stroke patients. The study involved 715 patients treated with Solitaire stent retrievers in three size configurations: 4×20 (28%), 4×40 (38%), and 6×30 (34%).

Primary endpoints assessed:
- First-pass effect (FPE): "achieving near-complete revascularization (modified Thrombolysis in Cerebral Infarction ≥2c) after the first pass"
- Functional independence at 90 days using modified Rankin Scale (0-2)

RESULTS:
The 4×40 stent retriever demonstrated superior early revascularization:
- Highest FPE rates compared to 6×30 (p=0.003) and modified FPE rates superior to both 4×20 and 6×30 groups
- "Use of the longer stent retriever (4×40) was an independent predictor of achieving modified FPE"
- Final revascularization, functional dependence, and 90-day mortality showed no significant differences across size groups

Conclusion: The longer 4×40 stent retriever appeared most effective for achieving rapid clot removal, potentially improving early treatment success in stroke thrombectomy procedures.

--- [5] Middle Cerebral Artery M2 Thrombectomy in the STRATIS Registry (Stroke, 2021) ---
PMID 34311566 - STRATIS M2 (Middle Cerebral Artery M2 Thrombectomy)

ABSTRACT/OVERVIEW:
This observational study examined mechanical thrombectomy outcomes for middle cerebral artery M2 versus M1 occlusions using data from the STRATIS Registry, which tracked 984 acute ischemic stroke patients treated within 8 hours of symptom onset.

Patient Groups:
- M1 occlusions: 538 patients (54.7%)
- M2 occlusions: 170 patients (17.3%)

KEY FINDINGS:
Primary Results: M2 and M1 patients achieved comparable functional outcomes. Specifically, "rates of good functional outcome...and mortality...were similar between the 2 groups" at approximately 58-59% good outcomes and 14-15% mortality rates.

Reperfusion Success: Both groups demonstrated similarly high reperfusion rates, with "rates of successful reperfusion...similar between the groups (91% versus 95%)."

Safety Concern: "M2 patients had significantly increased rates of symptomatic ICH at 24 hours (4% versus 1%)," indicating a higher intracranial hemorrhage risk in the M2 group.

Clinical Implications: The authors concluded that while M2 thrombectomy produced comparable functional outcomes to M1 treatment, the increased hemorrhage risk warrants further investigation into whether mechanical thrombectomy benefits M2 occlusion patients.

--- [6] Variation in Vessel Size and Angiographic Outcomes Following Stent-Retriever Thr (Stroke: Vascular and Interventional Neurology, 2024) ---
DOI 10.1161/SVIN.123.000978 - STRATIS Vessel Size: Redirects to AHA Journals, access denied (403 error).

--- [7] Stent-Retriever Thrombectomy after Intravenous t-PA vs. t-PA Alone in Stroke (New England Journal of Medicine, 2015) ---
During a 2-year period at four centers in Catalonia, Spain, 206 patients who could be treated within 8 hours after the onset of symptoms of acute ischemic stroke were randomly assigned to receive either medical therapy (including intravenous alteplase when eligible) and endovascular therapy with the Solitaire stent retriever (thrombectomy group) or medical therapy alone (control group). All patients had confirmed proximal anterior circulation occlusion and the absence of a large infarct on neuroimaging. The primary outcome was the severity of global disability at 90 days. Among patients with anterior circulation stroke who could be treated within 8 hours after symptom onset, stent retriever thrombectomy reduced the severity of post-stroke disability and increased the rate of functional independence (43.7% versus 28.2% in controls). Symptomatic intracranial hemorrhage occurred at 1.9% in both groups.

--- [8] A Randomized Trial of Intraarterial Treatment for Acute Ischemic Stroke (New England Journal of Medicine, 2015) ---
HERMES Meta-Analysis - Background: The study pooled data from five 2015 randomized trials examining endovascular thrombectomy for acute ischemic stroke caused by proximal anterior circulation artery occlusion. Methods: Researchers from the HERMES collaboration analyzed individual patient data from five trials (MR CLEAN, ESCAPE, REVASCAT, SWIFT PRIME, and EXTEND IA) conducted between December 2010 and December 2014. The primary outcome measured disability using the modified Rankin Scale at 90 days. Results: Endovascular thrombectomy produced significantly improved outcomes, with adjusted common odds ratio 2.49 (95% confidence interval 1.76-3.53; p<0.0001) compared to standard care. The number needed to treat was 2.6 patients to achieve one-level disability improvement for a single patient. Population Analyzed: 1,287 patients total (634 thrombectomy, 653 control). Subgroup Efficacy: Treatment benefits appeared consistent across diverse populations, with no heterogeneity of treatment effect across prespecified subgroups (p interaction=0.43). Notable effectiveness included elderly patients 80+ years and those treated beyond 300 minutes post-symptom onset. Safety: Mortality, parenchymal hematoma, and symptomatic intracranial hemorrhage rates did not differ significantly between groups. Conclusion: The analysis demonstrated endovascular thrombectomy benefits most stroke patients with large-vessel occlusion, regardless of demographics or geography.

--- [9] Randomized Assessment of Rapid Endovascular Treatment of Ischemic Stroke (New England Journal of Medicine, 2015) ---
SWIFT Trial - Background: The Solitaire Flow Restoration Device is a novel, self-expanding stent retriever designed to yield rapid flow restoration in acute cerebral ischaemia. Researchers compared this device against the Merci Retrieval System, the established mechanical thrombectomy standard. Methods: This randomized, parallel-group, non-inferiority trial enrolled 113 patients across 18 sites (17 USA, 1 France). Participants had acute ischemic stroke with moderate-to-severe deficits, treatable within 8 hours. The primary endpoint measured TIMI scale 2-3 blood flow without symptomatic intracranial hemorrhage after up to three device passes. Results: Primary Efficacy Outcome - Solitaire: 61% achieved target flow; Merci: 24% achieved target flow; Difference 37% [95% CI 19-53], odds ratio 4.87. 3-Month Neurological Outcomes - Solitaire: 58% showed good outcomes; Merci: 33% showed good outcomes; Difference 25% [6-43], odds ratio 2.78. Mortality at 90 Days - Solitaire: 17%; Merci: 38%; Difference -21% [-39 to -3], odds ratio 0.34. The trial demonstrated Solitaire's superior angiographic, safety, and clinical performance, leading to early termination via prespecified efficacy stopping rule.

--- [10] Endovascular Therapy for Ischemic Stroke with Perfusion-Imaging Selection (New England Journal of Medicine, 2015) ---
Background: The researchers investigated whether advanced imaging selection, newer devices, and early treatment could improve outcomes in endovascular stroke therapy, as prior trials showed variable results. Methods: Seventy patients with ischemic stroke within 4.5 hours of onset received either endovascular thrombectomy with a Solitaire FR stent retriever or alteplase alone. All participants had "occlusion of the internal carotid or middle cerebral artery" with salvageable brain tissue confirmed on CT perfusion imaging. Results: The trial stopped early due to efficacy. Endovascular therapy achieved superior reperfusion (median 100% vs. 37%, p<0.001) and improved early neurologic recovery at three days (80% versus 37%). Importantly, "more patients achieving functional independence (score of 0 to 2 on the modified Rankin scale, 71% vs. 40%; P=0.01)" at 90 days. No significant differences emerged in mortality or symptomatic bleeding rates. Conclusions: For patients with proximal arterial occlusion and salvageable tissue on imaging, early thrombectomy combined with initial alteplase "improved reperfusion, early neurologic recovery, and functional outcome" compared to alteplase monotherapy.

--- [11] Thrombectomy within 8 Hours after Symptom Onset in Ischemic Stroke (New England Journal of Medicine, 2015) ---
Background: The study tested whether advanced imaging selection, newer devices, and earlier intervention improve outcomes in endovascular stroke therapy. Methods: Researchers randomly assigned patients with ischemic stroke (within 4.5 hours of onset, receiving alteplase) to either endovascular thrombectomy with Solitaire FR stent retriever or alteplase alone. All participants had proximal arterial occlusion with salvageable tissue and ischemic core <70 ml on CT perfusion imaging. Key Outcomes: The trial stopped early after 70 patients due to efficacy. The endovascular group showed superior reperfusion at 24 hours (100% vs. 37%), greater early neurologic improvement at 3 days (80% vs. 37%), and better functional independence at 90 days (71% vs. 40%). There were no significant differences in rates of death or symptomatic intracerebral hemorrhage. Conclusion: Early thrombectomy combined with alteplase, guided by CT perfusion imaging selection, significantly improved reperfusion, neurologic recovery, and functional outcomes compared to alteplase monotherapy in patients with proximal cerebral artery occlusion.

--- [12] Endovascular thrombectomy after large-vessel ischaemic stroke: a meta-analysis o (The Lancet, 2016) ---
The HERMES collaboration pooled individual patient data from five randomized trials (MR CLEAN, ESCAPE, REVASCAT, SWIFT PRIME, and EXTEND IA) conducted between December 2010 and December 2014. The analysis included 1,287 patients (634 assigned to endovascular thrombectomy, 653 assigned to control) with acute ischemic stroke caused by occlusion of the proximal anterior circulation artery. Thrombectomy significantly reduced disability compared to control (adjusted odds ratio 2.49, p<0.0001). The number needed to treat was 2.6. Benefits persisted across subgroups, including patients aged 80+, those treated >300 minutes after symptom onset, and those ineligible for alteplase. Mortality and hemorrhage rates showed no significant differences. Endovascular thrombectomy is of benefit to most patients with acute ischemic stroke caused by occlusion of the proximal anterior circulation.

--- [13] Safety and Efficacy of Solitaire Stent Thrombectomy: Individual Patient Data Met (Stroke, 2016) ---
The SEER meta-analysis combined individual patient data from four randomized trials (SWIFT PRIME, ESCAPE, EXTEND-IA, and REVASCAT) where Solitaire was the primary or only device used. The analysis involved 787 patients total: 401 receiving endovascular thrombectomy and 386 receiving standard care. The treatment demonstrated substantial benefit with a common odds ratio of 2.7 for improved outcomes. Approximately 40 of 100 treated patients experienced reduced disability, with 23 achieving independent functional status. The number needed to treat was 2.5 patients to improve disability by one level. Successful revascularization occurred in 77% of Solitaire-treated patients. No significant differences emerged in symptomatic hemorrhage or mortality rates between groups. Benefits remained consistent across all patient subgroups tested. Solitaire thrombectomy proved safe and highly effective with substantially reduced disability for large vessel ischemic stroke.

--- [14] North American SOLITAIRE Stent-Retriever Acute Stroke Registry (Stroke, 2013) ---
An investigator-initiated NASA registry recruited 24 clinical sites within North America to submit demographic, clinical, site-adjudicated angiographic, and clinical outcome data on consecutive patients treated with the Solitaire Flow Restoration device. The study examined 354 patients treated at 24 centers between March 2012 and February 2013. Recanalization success (TIMI ≥2) was achieved in 83.3% in NASA versus 83% in SWIFT. Modified Rankin Scale ≤2 at 90 days was observed in 42% of NASA patients versus 37% (SWIFT) and 40% (TREVO 2). 90-day mortality was 30.2% in NASA versus 17.2% (SWIFT) and 29% (TREVO 2). The investigators determined that the Solitaire FR device performance in clinical practice is comparable with the SWIFT and TREVO 2 trial results, demonstrating the device's effectiveness outside controlled trial settings.

--- [15] Solitaire flow restoration device versus the Merci Retriever in patients with ac (International Journal of Stroke, 2012) ---
This randomized non-inferiority trial enrolled 113 patients across 18 centers (primarily in the USA) with acute ischemic stroke requiring treatment within 8 hours. Patients received either Solitaire or Merci thrombectomy devices. The Solitaire group demonstrated superior performance: 61% achieved the primary efficacy endpoint versus 24% in the Merci group (odds ratio 4.87). Three-month neurological outcomes favored Solitaire (58% vs 33%), and mortality was lower with Solitaire (17% vs 38%). The Solitaire Flow Restoration Device achieved substantially better angiographic, safety, and clinical outcomes than did the Merci Retrieval System.

--- [16] Mechanical thrombectomy in acute stroke: prospective pilot trial of the solitair (American Journal of Neuroradiology, 2012) ---
This prospective, single-center trial evaluated mechanical thrombectomy feasibility, safety, and efficacy in patients with acute ischemic stroke using the Solitaire FR device under conscious sedation. Patients with large artery occlusions were treated within 6 hours (anterior circulation) or 24 hours (posterior circulation) of symptom onset. Among 36 patients treated between May 2010 and July 2011, successful revascularization occurred in 28 patients (77.8%). At 3 months, 22 patients (61.1%) achieved good functional outcomes (modified Rankin Scale ≤2). The procedure was technically feasible in 86.1% of cases, though the overall mortality rate reached 22.2%. Mechanical thrombectomy while under conscious sedation is feasible in a large percentage of cases and demonstrates favorable functional outcomes.

--- [17] Solitaire FR thrombectomy system: immediate results in 56 consecutive acute isch (Journal of NeuroInterventional Surgery, 2018) ---
This study treated 56 consecutive acute stroke patients using intra-arterial therapy with the Solitaire FR system. The device successfully achieved recanalization in 50 of 56 patients (89%) with adequate TICI scores. Complications occurred in 5 patients, including two asymptomatic subarachnoid hemorrhages, two thromboembolic events, and one symptomatic intracranial hemorrhage. At discharge, 53.5% showed significant neurological improvement, and 46% achieved favorable functional outcomes. Solitaire FR is successful in achieving a high rate of arterial recanalization with a low complication rate, positioning it as an effective and safe thrombectomy option.

--- [18] Double solitaire mechanical thrombectomy in acute stroke: effective rescue strat (American Journal of Neuroradiology, 2014) ---
The Penumbra Pivotal Stroke Trial was a prospective, multicenter, single-arm study assessing safety and effectiveness of the Penumbra System for treating acute ischemic stroke caused by intracranial large vessel occlusive disease. The trial enrolled 125 patients with significant neurological deficits presenting within 8 hours of symptom onset. Participants had angiographically confirmed vessel occlusions and were either ineligible for or resistant to clot-dissolving medication. Successful revascularization occurred in approximately 82% of treated vessels. The procedure generated 18 adverse events in 16 patients (12.8%), with 3 serious events. Imaging revealed brain bleeding in 28% of patients within 24 hours, with symptomatic cases in 11.2%. Mortality reached 32.8% at 90 days, though 25% achieved favorable functional outcomes. The Penumbra System demonstrated safe and effective revascularization for stroke patients with large vessel blockages treated within the 8-hour window.

--- [19] Mechanical thrombectomy with the Solitaire AB device in acute ischemic stroke of (Neurology Journal, 2016) ---
The ASTER trial was a randomized, open-label, blinded end-point clinical trial conducted in 8 comprehensive stroke centers in France comparing contact aspiration versus stent retriever techniques for treating acute ischemic stroke with large vessel occlusion. Patients who presented with acute ischemic stroke and a large vessel occlusion in the anterior circulation within 6 hours of symptom onset were included. Patients were randomly assigned to first-line contact aspiration (n = 192) or first-line stent retriever (n = 189) immediately prior to mechanical thrombectomy. The primary outcome was the proportion of patients with successful revascularization defined as a modified Thrombolysis in Cerebral Infarction score of 2b or 3 at the end of all endovascular procedures. Among 381 randomized patients (mean age 69.9 years), 363 completed the trial. Successful revascularization occurred in 85.4% of the aspiration group versus 83.1% of the stent retriever group (P=.53). First-line thrombectomy with contact aspiration compared with stent retriever did not result in an increased successful revascularization rate at the end of the procedure, suggesting comparable effectiveness between the two techniques.

--- [20] Impact of age and baseline NIHSS scores on clinical outcomes in the mechanical t (Journal of NeuroInterventional Surgery, 2014) ---
The COMPASS trial was a multicentre randomized trial evaluating whether direct aspiration as first pass treatment was non-inferior to stent retriever therapy for acute ischemic stroke from large-vessel occlusion. This trial enrolled 270 patients at 15 sites (10 US hospitals, 4 specialty clinics, 1 Canadian hospital). Participants had anterior circulation large-vessel occlusion within 6 hours of symptom onset. Patients were randomly assigned to either direct aspiration first pass or stent retriever first line thrombectomy. The primary outcome measured functional recovery at 90 days using the modified Rankin Scale (0-2 score), with a non-inferiority margin of 0.15. Aspiration achieved a modified Rankin score of 0-2 in 52% of patients versus 50% in the stent retriever group, demonstrating non-inferiority. Intracranial hemorrhage occurred in 36% of aspiration patients and 34% of stent retriever patients. Three-month mortality was 22% in both groups. The results support the use of direct aspiration as an alternative to stent retriever as first-line therapy for stroke thrombectomy.

--- [21] Intracranial thrombectomy using the Solitaire stent: a historical vignette (Journal of NeuroInterventional Surgery, 2012) ---
A 67-year-old woman presented 3.5 hours after stroke onset with a right middle cerebral artery occlusion. Initial mechanical thrombectomy with a microbrush failed to achieve recanalization. The medical team then deployed a Solitaire stent within the blocked vessel. After several minutes of incubation, the expanded stent was slowly withdrawn under continuous aspiration with instantaneous removal of the entire thrombus and complete recanalization of the right MCA. The patient achieved a National Institutes of Health Stroke Scale score of 0 at 30-day follow-up, indicating complete recovery. Although initially designed for aneurysm treatment, the Solitaire stent proved safe and effective for thrombectomy, representing the first successful human clinical use of a Solitaire stent for this purpose and the ignition spark for the development of a whole generation of new devices, now called stent retrievers.

--- [22] Mechanical thrombectomy using a Solitaire stent retriever in the treatment of pe (Journal of NeuroInterventional Surgery, 2019) ---
A prospective, single-center pilot study evaluated the safety and effectiveness of a self-expanding and fully retrievable stent (Solitaire AB) in revascularization of patients with acute ischemic stroke. The study included 20 patients with acute ischemic stroke attributable to a large artery occlusion of the anterior circulation within the first 8 hours from symptoms onset. Successful revascularization defined as thrombosis in cerebral ischemia grade 2b or 3 was achieved in 18 of 20 (90%) treated vessels, and 16 patients showed immediate restoration of flow after stent deployment. The average number of passes for maximal recanalization was 1.4, and the median time from groin puncture to recanalization was 50 (38-71) minutes. The Solitaire AB device can rapidly, safely, and effectively retrieve clots from major brain arteries within the treatment window.

--- [23] Solitaire stentectomy: 'deploy and engage' and 'loop and snare' techniques (Journal of NeuroInterventional Surgery, 2016) ---
This article discusses mechanical thrombectomy using Solitaire FR stents for acute ischemic stroke caused by large vessel blockage. A significant complication is stent detachment during the procedure, which is associated with poor technical and clinical outcome. The paper presents two patient cases where conventional snare retrieval failed, prompting alternative techniques: the deploy and engage method and the loop and snare technique. Both approaches proved successful in removing the detached stents without requiring medications that could worsen acute stroke outcomes. The key advantage of retrieval techniques is the lack of need to administer fibrinolytic/antiplatelet agents that can potentially be catastrophic in the setting of acute stroke.

--- [24] Prospective, Multi-Centre, Single-Arm Study of Mechanical Thrombectomy using Sol (Stroke, 2013) ---
STAR was an international, multicenter, prospective, single-arm study of Solitaire FR thrombectomy in patients with large vessel anterior circulation strokes. A total of 202 patients were enrolled across 14 comprehensive stroke centers. Successful revascularization was achieved in 79.2% of patients. Favorable neurological outcomes occurred in 57.9% of participants, with device-related severe complications in 7.4% of cases. Mortality reached 6.9%, while intracranial bleeding occurred in 18.8% of patients, with only 1.5% being symptomatic. Solitaire FR treatment results in high rates of revascularization, low risk of clinically relevant procedural complications, and good clinical outcomes in combination with low mortality at 90 days.

--- [25] Technical Feasibility and Application of Mechanical Thrombectomy with the Solita (American Journal of Neuroradiology, 2013) ---
This study evaluated 14 consecutive patients with basilar artery occlusion (BAO) treated using the Solitaire FR Revascularization Device, often combined with thromboaspiration, thrombolysis, or additional stent placement. Overall, successful recanalization (TICI 3 or 2b) was achieved in all patients (TICI 3 in 78.6%, 11/14). Average device passes were 1.3, and median procedure time was 47 minutes. No device-related complications or symptomatic intracranial hemorrhage occurred. Good functional outcome (modified Rankin Scale 0-2) was achieved in 28.6% of patients. The multimodal endovascular approach demonstrated high recanalization rates with minimal complications, though underlying atherosclerotic lesions sometimes required permanent stent placement for complete vessel opening.

--- [26] Complications of Endovascular Treatment for Acute Stroke in the SWIFT Trial with (American Journal of Neuroradiology, 2013) ---
Twenty-three centers enrolled 634 consecutive patients treated between March 2013 and August 2015 using the Trevo device in the TRACK registry. Mean age was 66.1±14.8 years and mean baseline NIHSS score was 17.4±6.7; 86.7% had anterior circulation occlusions. The primary outcome was achieving a Thrombolysis in Cerebral Infarction (TICI) score of ≥2b. Among the 634 patients, 80.3% achieved successful revascularization. Forty-seven percent achieved favorable 90-day outcomes. Within guideline-recommended parameters (anterior circulation, 6-hour window), outcomes reached 51.4%. Mortality was 19.8%. The TRACK Registry validates that recent thrombectomy trial benefits translate to real-world practice, with treatment outcomes showing no significant differences between guideline-adherent and non-adherent cases.

--- [27] The INSPIRE Registry: Entering a New Era of Medical Device Research in the Neuro (Clinical Neuroradiology, 2020) ---
The Innovative Neurovascular Product Surveillance Registry (INSPIRE) is a unique, large scale, multitherapy, long-term observational study platform, sponsored by Medtronic Neurovascular, designed to continuously monitor the safety and performance of newly commercialized devices in real-world clinical settings. The first phase focuses on flow diverter devices (Pipeline Flex and Shield) with over 700 patients enrolled across 30 international sites. A second phase is enrolling stroke patients treated with Solitaire X and React 68/71 devices. Patients are prospectively enrolled from selected international sites whenever treated with the sponsor's new neurovascular devices, with all study data based on routine standard of care. The registry represents a collaborative approach between industry and the medical community, aligning with European medical device regulations regarding postmarketing surveillance.

--- [28] Solitaire Flow Restoration thrombectomy for acute ischemic stroke: retrospective (Journal of Neurointerventional Surgery, 2013) ---
STORM-PE CAVT mechanical thrombectomy trial: This trial evaluated computer-assisted vacuum thrombectomy (CAVT) combined with anticoagulation versus anticoagulation alone for intermediate-high risk pulmonary embolism. The study enrolled 100 normotensive patients with acute PE (symptoms ≤14 days), elevated RV/LV ratios on imaging, and elevated cardiac biomarkers across 22 international sites. Results: The CAVT group demonstrated superior improvement in RV/LV ratio reduction (0.52±0.37) compared to anticoagulation alone (0.24±0.40), with a mean difference of 0.27 (95% CI, 0.12-0.43; P<0.001). Modified Miller scores showed greater improvements with CAVT versus anticoagulation alone at 48 hours (P<0.001). Early normalization of vital signs within 48 hours occurred more frequently following CAVT treatment. Major adverse event rates at 7 days were comparable between groups: CAVT 4.3% versus anticoagulation 7.5% (P=0.681). However, two PE-related deaths occurred in the CAVT arm.

data.Press Release Title: HERMES: Pooled Individual Patient Data from 5 Trials Shows Endovascular Thrombectomy Reduces Disability (Solitaire Primary Device)
data.Press Release Content: HERMES Trial: Meta-Analysis of Endovascular Thrombectomy. Study Overview: The HERMES collaboration pooled individual patient data from five randomized trials (MR CLEAN, ESCAPE, REVASCAT, SWIFT PRIME, and EXTEND IA) conducted between December 2010 and December 2014. Patient Population: The analysis examined 1,287 patients—634 received endovascular thrombectomy, 653 received standard care—all with acute ischemic stroke from proximal anterior circulation vessel occlusion. Primary Clinical Result: Treatment with endovascular thrombectomy demonstrated substantial benefit with adjusted cOR 2.49, 95% CI 1.76-3.53; p<0.0001 for reduced disability at 90 days. The number needed to treat was 2.6 to prevent one additional patient from experiencing disability progression. Key Subgroup Findings: The intervention proved effective across diverse populations including patients aged 80+ years (cOR 3.68), benefit persisted when treatment occurred beyond 300 minutes post-symptom (cOR 1.76), and those ineligible for intravenous alteplase still benefited (cOR 2.43). Safety Profile: Mortality at 90 days and risk of parenchymal haematoma and symptomatic intracranial haemorrhage did not differ between populations. Clinical Significance: Results demonstrated no heterogeneity of treatment effect across subgroups, establishing endovascular thrombectomy as beneficial for most patients with this stroke type, regardless of demographics or geography.
