Multiple stent retrievers comparison systematic review: The study aimed to systematically review and compare findings from meta-analyses evaluating the direct aspiration first pass technique (ADAPT) versus stent retrievers for acute ischemic stroke treatment. Seven studies met inclusion criteria. Results: The analysis revealed inconsistent methodologies across existing meta-analyses comparing these thrombectomy approaches. The authors concluded that present evidence is insufficient to clarify the superiority of one modality over the other. They emphasized the necessity for additional randomized controlled trials before drawing definitive conclusions about which technique performs better. The researchers also noted that future investigations should incorporate procedure duration and cost-effectiveness considerations.

data.Press Release Title: Terumo Neuro Announces EMEA Commercial Availability of SOFIA Flow 88 Neurovascular Aspiration Catheter
data.Press Release Content:
Terumo Neuro SOFIA Flow 88 Announcement Summary

Key Announcement
Terumo Neuro announced EMEA commercial availability of the SOFIA Flow 88 Neurovascular Aspiration Catheter on June 2, 2025, expanding its stroke treatment portfolio.

Device Details
Specification: 0.088-inch inner diameter large-bore catheter
Key Features:
- Reliable trackability through challenging anatomy
- Proximal stability for procedural ease with short and long sheaths
- Atraumatic design with flexibility throughout working length
- Compliant SOFIA tip that conforms to clots
- Nitinol braid and coil reinforcement to maintain lumen integrity

Clinical Legacy
The broader SOFIA Catheter line has supported over 500,000 procedures across 170 countries over the past decade, backed by multiple published clinical trials and registries.

Clinical Evidence
- SOFAST registry and SESAME trial data demonstrate strong results in speed, efficacy, and safety
- Compatible with SOFIA Plus 6F Aspiration Catheter

Regulatory Status
- Class III CE Marked (0297)
- Intended for healthcare professionals only

Executive Quote
As we introduce the SOFIA Flow 88 Neurovascular Aspiration Catheter, we are buildi... — Carsten Schroeder, President and CEO

Event Location
Showcased at LINNC Paris 2025, booth 8 at Le Carrousel du Louvre

data.Press Release Title: MicroVention Reshapes Stroke Therapy with Introduction of ERIC Retrieval Device
data.Press Release Content:
ERIC Retrieval Device Announcement Summary

Announcement Details
Date: June 27, 2023
Location: Aliso Viejo, California
Company: MicroVention, Inc. (Terumo Corporation subsidiary)

Device Overview
The ERIC Retrieval Device is a self-expanding, laser-cut clot retriever designed for ischemic stroke treatment. It features multiple retrieval cages on a pusher wire delivery system and offers:
- Thrombus control via cage-like design (unlike open-ended tubular stents)
- Atraumatic design with soft radial force and minimal vessel contact
- Three sizes treating vessel ranges from 5.5 mm to 1.5 mm
- Compatibility with HEADWAY 17 and HEADWAY 21 Microcatheters
- No clot integration wait time

Regulatory Status
- FDA 510(k) Clearance: March 31, 2022
- Commercial Availability: June 2023 in the United States

Clinical Experience
First U.S. cases performed at Riverside Methodist Hospital (Columbus, OH) in February 2023 by Dr. Pema, Dr. Alhajeri, and Dr. Budzik.

Key Quote
Dr. Adel Malek (Tufts Medical Center) stated the device's low-profile design facilitates synergistic use with SOFIA catheters and reduces time to recanalization.

Clinical Results
ERIC demonstrated safe, effective outcomes with results comparable to other retrieval devices regarding successful revascularization and clinical safety outcomes.

data.Press Release Title: MicroVention Announces Next Evolution in Stent Innovation: LVIS EVO Now Available in US
data.Press Release Content:
LVIS EVO Intraluminal Support Device Announcement

Key Announcement
MicroVention announced on June 26, 2024, that the LVIS EVO device is now commercially available in the United States for treating wide-neck intracranial aneurysms.

Device Details
The LVIS EVO features:
- Full visibility of the stent body under fluoroscopy
- DFT wire construction with advanced braid design
- Ability to resheath the device up to 80% of its length
- Optimized braid angle for improved device opening
- Compatibility with Headway 17 Advanced Microcatheter and Scepter C/XC Occlusion Balloons

Clinical Data
- Over 12,000 units sold in Europe since 2019 launch
- First fully visible coil-assist-intracranial stent available in the US market

Indications for Use (FDA)
For patients ≥18 years with wide-neck (≥4 mm neck width or dome-to-neck ratio <2) saccular intracranial aneurysms in parent vessels 2.0-4.5 mm in diameter.

Key Quote
Dr. Charles Matouk, Yale University neurosurgery vice chair, stated: With improved visibility and the ability to shelf the stent across the aneurysm neck, it's become my tool-of-choice.

Company Background
MicroVention is a Terumo Corporation subsidiary founded in 1997, headquartered in Aliso Viejo, California.

data.Press Release Title: Terumo Neuro Announces US Commercial Availability of SOFIA 88 Neurovascular Support Catheter
data.Press Release Content:
SOFIA 88 Neurovascular Support Catheter Announcement

Announcement Date: May 13, 2025

Device: SOFIA 88 Neurovascular Support Catheter - a large-bore catheter with 0.088-inch inner diameter for stroke treatment

Key Features:
- Reliable trackability through challenging anatomy
- Proximal stability with short and long sheaths
- Atraumatic design minimizing vessel trauma

Clinical Background:
The SOFIA catheter line has supported over 500,000 procedures across 170 countries during the past decade. The new device is compatible with the SOFIA Flow Plus 6F Aspiration Catheter.

Clinical Data:
The SOFAST registry evaluated SOFIA Flow Plus 6F as a first-line approach and demonstrated strong results across speed, efficacy, and safety.

Key Quote:
Dr. Ameer Hassan (Valley Baptist, University of Texas Rio Grande Valley) stated the catheter has great trackability and it is improving my access whether I decide to use it to support an aspiration only or a combination approach.

Company Leadership:
Carsten Schroeder, President and CEO of Terumo Neuro, emphasized the launch reflects continued investment in neurovascular innovation through physician collaboration.

About Terumo Neuro:
Founded in 1997 as MicroVention, acquired by Terumo Corporation in 2006. Offers 30+ products for cerebral aneurysms, ischemic stroke, carotid artery disease, and neurovascular malformations.

data.Press Release Title: Terumo Neuro Unveils New SOFIA Flow 88 Data from EMEA Demonstrating Strong Performance (ESMINT 2025)
data.Press Release Content:
Terumo Neuro SOFIA Flow 88 Announcement Summary

Device Details
The SOFIA Flow 88 is a neurovascular aspiration catheter designed for mechanical thrombectomy procedures. It features a super bore design to maximize aspiration while minimizing flow disruption, with reliable trackability and vessel-friendly flexibility.

Clinical Data (EMEA Physician Preference Study)
Study Parameters:
- 70 procedures across 16 hospitals
- 31 physicians evaluated the device
- Data presented at ESMINT Congress (September 3, 2025)

Key Findings:
- 83% of physicians reported the catheter reliably reached target locations, even in challenging anatomies (76% in ICA-T or beyond)
- 100% rated trackability same or better than SOFIA Plus 6F
- 92% rated overall performance same, better, or much better than alternative aspiration catheters

Commercial Availability
Launched in EMEA on June 2, 2025

Key Quote
Its super bore design maximizes aspiration and minimizes flow disruption while delivering exceptional support, stated Carsten Schroeder, President and CEO of Terumo Neuro.

Supporting Product Portfolio
Compatible with SOFIA Plus 6F and integrated with devices including ERIC Retrieval Device, BOBBY Balloon Guide Catheter, WEDGE/HEADWAY Microcatheters, and TRAXCESS Guidewires.

feature_claims.text: The BENCHMARK BMX96 is a large bore access catheter designed to provide stable vascular access during neurovascular procedures. It serves as a primary access platform for aspiration thrombectomy procedures and intracranial device delivery. The BMX96 is characterized by its large inner lumen diameter, enabling maximum aspiration force delivery during mechanical thrombectomy interventions.

The BENCHMARK BMX96 is used in conjunction with Penumbra aspiration thrombectomy systems, including the Penumbra ENGINE aspiration system. Clinical utility is supported through integration with proven aspiration thrombectomy protocols and systems. The large lumen design is optimized for maximum aspiration force generation in acute stroke intervention.
- Large lumen provides maximum aspiration force capability
- Enables efficient blood clot removal

data.Press Release Title: Penumbra Introduces Latest Stroke Thrombectomy Aspiration Technology at SNIS 2019 (JET 7 with JET D)
data.Press Release Content:
Penumbra JET 7 XTRA FLEX Technology Announcement

Announcement Details:
Penumbra announced commercial availability of the JET 7 reperfusion catheter with XTRA FLEX technology at the Society of NeuroInterventional Surgery (SNIS) 16th Annual Meeting in Miami, USA on July 22, 2019.

Device Specifications:
The JET 7 represents the eighth-generation reperfusion catheter in the Penumbra System designed for proximal large vessel occlusions. Key features include advanced trackability, improved navigation capabilities, and maintenance of a large 0.072" lumen diameter. The device works in conjunction with the Penumbra ENGINE as part of an aspiration-based mechanical thrombectomy system.

Clinical Application:
The integrated system is designed to extract thrombus in acute ischemic stroke patients, with the combination of JET 7 and ENGINE technology intended to maximise thrombus removal force.

Clinical Presentation:
Donald F Frei, director of Neurointerventional Surgery at Swedish Medical Center in Englewood, shared initial clinical experience with the technology during the SNIS meeting presentation on July 22nd.

Product Timeline:
The JET 7 XTRA FLEX represents the latest advancement following the September 2018 launch of the JET-ENGINE family of products.

data.Article Abstracts & Results:
--- [1] The Penumbra Pivotal Stroke Trial: Safety and Effectiveness of a New Generation  (Stroke, 2009) ---
Penumbra SMART Coil System clinical evaluation: Background: The study aimed to evaluate long-term clinical outcomes for arteriovenous fistula (AVF) patients treated with the Penumbra SMART COIL System through a prospective, multicenter registry analysis. Methods: This subset analysis included AVF patients who underwent endovascular coiling using the SMART COIL system, with monitoring for 12 ± 6 months following treatment. Results: The analysis encompassed 41 patients. Safety: Zero procedural device-related serious adverse events occurred; periprocedural SAE rate was 2.4% (1/41), and no periprocedural deaths occurred. Technical Success: Complete occlusion was achieved in 87.8% (36/41) of patients, with 85.4% (35/41) of the patients avoiding catheter kickout complications. Follow-up Outcomes: At 1 year, the lesion occlusion was better or stable in 93.3% (28/30) of patients. Retreatment: Only 3.4% (1/29) of patients required retreatment during follow-up. Adverse Events: The SAE rate from 24 hours to one year was 26.8%, with 1-year all-cause mortality rate of 2.4% (1/41). Functional Status: At one-year follow-up, 90.9% (20/22) of patients had a modified Rankin Scale score within the range of 0 to 2.

--- [2] Effect of Endovascular Contact Aspiration vs Stent Retriever on Revascularizatio (JAMA, 2017) ---
SOFIA Plus catheter clinical outcomes SESAME/SOFAST registry: No PubMed article found matching this exact search.

--- [3] Aspiration thrombectomy versus stent retriever thrombectomy as first-line approa (The Lancet, 2019) ---
SOFIA catheter aspiration thrombectomy ASTER trial subanalysis: No PubMed article found matching this exact search.

--- [4] Endovascular Treatment of Acute Ischemic Stroke With the Penumbra System in Rout (Stroke, 2021) ---
SOFIA aspiration catheter real-world data: SOFIA 6F Plus Meta-Analysis Study: This research examined direct aspiration using SOFIA 6 F Plus catheters for large-vessel occlusion stroke treatment. The investigation included 144 consecutive patients plus a meta-analysis of nine articles totaling 758 patients. Results: Successful recanalization (mTICI 2b-3) rate of 75.7% was achieved with direct aspiration alone in the cohort. Functional independence at 90 days occurred in 40.3% of cases. Meta-analysis of pooled data revealed: mTICI 2b-3 rate was 71.6% (95%CI, 66.3-76.5%). Rescue stent-retriever backup was needed in 24.1% of cases. Overall success: mTICI 2b-3 rate after DA and rescue therapy was 88.9%. Functional independence: A pooled estimate of 45.6% achieved favorable outcomes. Mortality: 19% within 90 days. Hemorrhage risk: 5.8% (95%CI, 4.2-8.0%) of symptomatic intracranial hemorrhage.

--- [5] Aspiration thrombectomy with the Penumbra System for patients with stroke and la (Frontiers in Neurology, 2023) ---
ERIC retrieval device clinical evaluation: Background: The study evaluated safety and efficacy of mechanical thrombectomy using the ERIC device for acute ischemic stroke patients with distal medium vessel occlusions. Methods: A retrospective analysis examined 50 patients from a prospectively collected database, documenting clinical patient characteristics, procedural measures, timestamps at each stage, and patient outcomes. Results: The cohort included equal gender distribution (50% male/female) with median age 67 years and median NIHSS score of 14. First-pass recanalization outcomes showed: eTICI3 in 32%, eTICI-2c in 16%, eTICI-2B67 in 10%, eTICI2B50 in 6%, and mTICI 0-2A in 36%. The final recanalization rate achieved mTICI 2b-3 in 90% of cases and mTICI 2c-3 in 70%. Conclusion: The ERIC thrombectomy device offers a high rate of first-pass success along with a favorable safety profile.

--- [6] First-Pass Effect With Aspiration Thrombectomy With the Penumbra System in Patie (Stroke: Vascular and Interventional Neurology, 2022) ---
ERIC retrieval device multicenter study: No additional specific multicenter ERIC study found beyond Row 70.

--- [7] Penumbra, Inc. Announces Completion of THUNDER IDE Study Enrollment for Acute Is (Press Release, 2024) ---
Headway microcatheter clinical performance: The Headway27 microcatheter features optimal dimensions: 156 cm length, 2.6 Fr distal OD, ID 0.027 inches with excellent visibility for stroke procedures. Results: The study demonstrated strong clinical performance in 50 consecutive thrombectomy cases. Thrombectomy approaches: Manual aspiration alone occurred in 72% of cases, with stentriever-assisted techniques in 6% and combined methods in 22%. Recanalization success: mTICI 2B/3 recanalization was achieved in 94% of cases. Time efficiency: Median groin puncture to final recanalization time was 27 minutes. Vessel access: The device successfully reached all intended target vessels across M1, M2, M3, P2, P3, and basilar artery locations. Complication rate: Only 2 intra-procedural complications occurred, neither microcatheter-related. Device reliability: There were no cases requiring usage of an additional or alternative microcatheter.

--- [8] Randomized Controlled Trial of Mechanical Thrombectomy With Anticoagulation Vers (Circulation, 2025) ---
Background: Intermediate-high risk pulmonary embolism patients face elevated risk of clinical deterioration and mortality. The trial tested mechanical thrombectomy combined with anticoagulation against anticoagulation therapy alone. Methods: This international randomized controlled trial enrolled 100 patients across 22 sites with acute PE (symptoms ≤14 days), normotension, RV/LV ratio ≥1.0, and elevated cardiac biomarkers. Participants received either computer-assisted vacuum thrombectomy (CAVT) with anticoagulation (n=47) or anticoagulation alone (n=53). The primary endpoint measured RV/LV ratio changes at 48 hours via blinded imaging assessment. Results: CAVT demonstrated significantly greater RV/LV ratio reduction (0.52±0.37 versus 0.24±0.40; P<0.001). Pulmonary artery obstruction scores improved more substantially with CAVT. Vital sign normalization occurred more frequently within 48 hours following CAVT. Major adverse events within 7 days showed comparable rates between groups: "CAVT, 4.3% versus anticoagulation, 7.5%" (P=0.681). Two PE-related deaths occurred in the CAVT arm. Conclusions: CAVT proved superior to anticoagulation monotherapy for reducing RV/LV ratio within 48 hours, with faster vital sign normalization and comparable safety profiles regarding major adverse events.

--- [9] The SMART Registry: Long-Term Results on the Utility of the Penumbra SMART COIL  (Neurosurgical Focus, 2021) ---
EmboTrap II EXCELLENT registry results: Background: The mTICI 2B score indicates 50-99% reperfusion but represents a broad range. Researchers investigated whether very late leptomeningeal collaterals (VLLC) could further differentiate outcomes in this patient group. Method: A prospective registry of anterior circulation acute ischemic stroke patients treated with EmboTrap devices (2013-2016) was analyzed. Results: Among 177 anterior circulation stroke patients, 94 (53.1%) achieved only mTICI-2B reperfusion. Within this group, 16/94 (17.0%) patients achieved excellent functional outcomes at 3 months and 26 (27.7%) had hyperdensity on follow-up computed tomography. On univariate analysis, VLLC presence was inversely associated with excellent functional outcomes and positively associated with mortality in mTICI-2B patients. Multivariate analysis showed VLLC was inversely associated with excellent outcomes (odds ratio 0.075, 95% CI 0.007-0.765, P = 0.029) but not significantly associated with mortality.

--- [10] Effect of Thrombectomy With Combined Contact Aspiration and Stent Retriever vs S (JAMA, 2021) ---
Background: The study addresses uncertainty about whether combining contact aspiration with stent retriever improves outcomes compared to stent retriever alone for treating acute ischemic stroke caused by large vessel occlusion in the anterior circulation. Methods: This multicenter, randomized controlled trial enrolled 408 patients (405 analyzed) from 11 French stroke centers between October 2017 and May 2018. Participants had anterior circulation large vessel occlusion within 8 hours of symptom onset. Patients were randomly assigned to receive either combined contact aspiration plus stent retriever (205 patients) or stent retriever alone (203 patients). The primary endpoint measured successful reperfusion using the expanded Thrombolysis In Cerebral Infarction score. Results: The combined approach did not significantly improve the primary outcome. Successful near-total/total reperfusion (eTICI 2c/3) occurred in 64.5% of the combined group versus 57.9% of the stent retriever-only group (P = .17). However, the combined technique showed better results for intermediate reperfusion measures after the initial intervention alone (86.2% vs 72.3%, P < .001). Conclusions: Initial thrombectomy using combined contact aspiration and stent retriever did not significantly improve the rate of near-total or total reperfusion compared with stent retriever monotherapy, though the trial may have lacked sufficient power to detect smaller differences.

--- [11] Initial Data Presented for Penumbra's INSIGHT Registry and Subanalysis of the CO (Endovascular Today, 2022) ---
LVIS stent for intracranial aneurysm treatment: The objective was to evaluate the feasibility, efficacy, and safety of the Low-profile Visualized Intraluminal Support (LVIS) device for stent-assisted coiling of wide-necked intracranial aneurysms. Results: Nine studies encompassing 384 patients and 390 aneurysms were analyzed. Technical Success: 96.8% (95% CI 94.4% to 99.1%) achievement rate. Complete Occlusion: 54.6% (95% CI 31.8% to 77.4%) immediately after treatment, improving to 84.3% (95% CI 78.9% to 89.7%) at follow-up. Safety Profile: Procedure-related morbidity was 1.4% (95% CI 0.2% to 2.6%) with zero mortality. Complications: Thromboembolic events occurred in 4.9% (95% CI 1.9% to 7.9%) of cases; hemorrhagic complications were 2.1% (95% CI 0.7% to 3.5%).

--- [12] Technical note and first results on JET 7 thromboaspiration device for T-ICA occ (BMC Neurology, 2022) ---
LVIS EVO stent clinical data: The LVIS EVO is a braided, self-expanding stent approved in Europe (2019) and the US (2024) for treating intracranial aneurysms. Researchers conducted a meta-analysis examining safety outcomes, occlusion rates, and technical success using data from PubMed, MEDLINE, and Embase through August 2024. The study included seven studies evaluating 422 patients with 435 aneurysms. Results: Technical success rate was 99.3% (95% CI, 98.5%-100%). Occlusion Rates: Immediate post-procedure: 75.5% (95% CI, 62.1%-89.0%); At follow-up (mean 256 days): 86.8% (95% CI, 80.4%-93.2%). Complications: Overall rate: 7.4% (95% CI, 2.6%-12.2%); Treatment-related morbidity: 1.8% (95% CI, 0.5%-3.0%); Ischemic-thromboembolic events: 4.7% (95% CI, 1.5%-8.0%); Symptomatic events: 2.2% (95% CI, 0.6%-3.8%).

data.Press Release Title: Penumbra Announces European Launch of RED Reperfusion Catheters for Stroke Care
data.Press Release Content:
Penumbra RED Reperfusion Catheters European Launch

Announcement Details
Penumbra announced that its RED reperfusion catheters have received CE mark approval and are now available commercially in Europe for acute ischemic stroke treatment.

Device Features
The RED catheter family incorporates:
- REDglide technology for enhanced navigation through complex brain vessel anatomy
- Full-length PTFE liner to maintain catheter diameter under vacuum pressure
- Engineering designed for trackability and aspiration efficiency across various large vessel occlusions

Clinical Data
Initial data indicates the catheters can restore blood flow quickly across all clot types, though specific efficacy metrics are not detailed in the announcement.

Key Quotes
Dr. Saleh Lamin (Queen Elizabeth Hospital, Birmingham): With RED catheters, we will be able to get up to the clot quickly and efficiently remove it, most of the time within a single pass.

Joan Kristensen (Penumbra EMEA VP): Enhanced trackability and powerful aspiration...would potentially have a significant impact on patient outcomes here in Europe.

Timeline
- Date: September 7, 2022

Regulatory Status
- CE mark secured (European approval)
- Product now commercially available in Europe

Patients were randomly assigned to receive either combined contact aspiration plus stent retriever (205 patients) or stent retriever alone (203 patients). The primary endpoint measured successful reperfusion using the expanded Thrombolysis In Cerebral Infarction score. Results: The combined approach did not significantly improve the primary outcome. Successful near-total/total reperfusion (eTICI 2c/3) occurred in 64.5% of the combined group versus 57.9% of the stent retriever-only group (P = .17). However, the combined technique showed better results for intermediate reperfusion measures after the initial intervention alone (86.2% vs 72.3%, P < .001). Conclusions: Initial thrombectomy using combined contact aspiration and stent retriever did not significantly improve the rate of near-total or total reperfusion compared with stent retriever monotherapy, though the trial may have lacked sufficient power to detect smaller differences.

data.Press Release Title: New Data Demonstrate Effectiveness of Penumbra System and RED Catheters for Broad Stroke Care (WFITN 2022)
data.Press Release Content:
Penumbra System and RED Catheters Show Strong Stroke Care Results

Announcement Details
Penumbra announced initial data from two clinical studies presented at the World Federation of Interventional and Therapeutic Neuroradiology (WFITN) 2022 congress in Kyoto, Japan (August 21-25, 2022).

Device Information
The announcement focused on Penumbra's RED (Reperfusion cathEter with Distal vessel access) reperfusion catheters, designed for mechanical thrombectomy in acute ischemic stroke treatment.

Clinical Data - INSIGHT Registry
A multicentre postmarket evaluation involving 161 acute ischemic stroke patients showed:
- 68.9% achieved successful first-pass revascularization (mTICI ≥2b-3)
- 97% achieved successful revascularization after final angiogram
- 98.1% of firm red clot patients and 100% of other clot types reached final mTICI ≥2b-3

Clinical Data - COMPLETE Study
Sub-analyses from the global prospective trial demonstrated:
- Tandem lesion patients: 89% achieved mTICI 2b-3; nearly 64% reached 90-day good functional outcomes
- Late-window patients: 83.2% successful revascularization; 55.4% achieved good 90-day outcomes
- No device-related adverse events observed in late-window cohort

Key Quote
Sandra Lesenfants, president of interventional at Penumbra, noted: the latest data demonstrate that our latest technology is indeed making a positive impact.

data.Article Abstracts & Results:
--- [1] The Penumbra Pivotal Stroke Trial: Safety and Effectiveness of a New Generation  (Stroke, 2009) ---
Penumbra SMART Coil System clinical evaluation: Background: The study aimed to evaluate long-term clinical outcomes for arteriovenous fistula (AVF) patients treated with the Penumbra SMART COIL System through a prospective, multicenter registry analysis. Methods: This subset analysis included AVF patients who underwent endovascular coiling using the SMART COIL system, with monitoring for 12 ± 6 months following treatment. Results: The analysis encompassed 41 patients. Safety: Zero procedural device-related serious adverse events occurred; periprocedural SAE rate was 2.4% (1/41), and no periprocedural deaths occurred. Technical Success: Complete occlusion was achieved in 87.8% (36/41) of patients, with 85.4% (35/41) of the patients avoiding catheter kickout complications. Follow-up Outcomes: At 1 year, the lesion occlusion was better or stable in 93.3% (28/30) of patients. Retreatment: Only 3.4% (1/29) of patients required retreatment during follow-up. Adverse Events: The SAE rate from 24 hours to one year was 26.8%, with 1-year all-cause mortality rate of 2.4% (1/41). Functional Status: At one-year follow-up, 90.9% (20/22) of patients had a modified Rankin Scale score within the range of 0 to 2.

--- [2] Effect of Endovascular Contact Aspiration vs Stent Retriever on Revascularizatio (JAMA, 2017) ---
SOFIA Plus catheter clinical outcomes SESAME/SOFAST registry: No PubMed article found matching this exact search.

--- [3] Aspiration thrombectomy versus stent retriever thrombectomy as first-line approa (The Lancet, 2019) ---
SOFIA catheter aspiration thrombectomy ASTER trial subanalysis: No PubMed article found matching this exact search.

--- [4] Endovascular Treatment of Acute Ischemic Stroke With the Penumbra System in Rout (Stroke, 2021) ---
SOFIA aspiration catheter real-world data: SOFIA 6F Plus Meta-Analysis Study: This research examined direct aspiration using SOFIA 6 F Plus catheters for large-vessel occlusion stroke treatment. The investigation included 144 consecutive patients plus a meta-analysis of nine articles totaling 758 patients. Results: Successful recanalization (mTICI 2b-3) rate of 75.7% was achieved with direct aspiration alone in the cohort. Functional independence at 90 days occurred in 40.3% of cases. Meta-analysis of pooled data revealed: mTICI 2b-3 rate was 71.6% (95%CI, 66.3-76.5%). Rescue stent-retriever backup was needed in 24.1% of cases. Overall success: mTICI 2b-3 rate after DA and rescue therapy was 88.9%. Functional independence: A pooled estimate of 45.6% achieved favorable outcomes. Mortality: 19% within 90 days. Hemorrhage risk: 5.8% (95%CI, 4.2-8.0%) of symptomatic intracranial hemorrhage.

--- [5] Aspiration thrombectomy with the Penumbra System for patients with stroke and la (Frontiers in Neurology, 2023) ---
ERIC retrieval device clinical evaluation: Background: The study evaluated safety and efficacy of mechanical thrombectomy using the ERIC device for acute ischemic stroke patients with distal medium vessel occlusions. Methods: A retrospective analysis examined 50 patients from a prospectively collected database, documenting clinical patient characteristics, procedural measures, timestamps at each stage, and patient outcomes. Results: The cohort included equal gender distribution (50% male/female) with median age 67 years and median NIHSS score of 14. First-pass recanalization outcomes showed: eTICI3 in 32%, eTICI-2c in 16%, eTICI-2B67 in 10%, eTICI2B50 in 6%, and mTICI 0-2A in 36%. The final recanalization rate achieved mTICI 2b-3 in 90% of cases and mTICI 2c-3 in 70%. Conclusion: The ERIC thrombectomy device offers a high rate of first-pass success along with a favorable safety profile.

--- [6] First-Pass Effect With Aspiration Thrombectomy With the Penumbra System in Patie (Stroke: Vascular and Interventional Neurology, 2022) ---
ERIC retrieval device multicenter study: No additional specific multicenter ERIC study found beyond Row 70.

--- [7] Penumbra, Inc. Announces Completion of THUNDER IDE Study Enrollment for Acute Is (Press Release, 2024) ---
Headway microcatheter clinical performance: The Headway27 microcatheter features optimal dimensions: 156 cm length, 2.6 Fr distal OD, ID 0.027 inches with excellent visibility for stroke procedures. Results: The study demonstrated strong clinical performance in 50 consecutive thrombectomy cases. Thrombectomy approaches: Manual aspiration alone occurred in 72% of cases, with stentriever-assisted techniques in 6% and combined methods in 22%. Recanalization success: mTICI 2B/3 recanalization was achieved in 94% of cases. Time efficiency: Median groin puncture to final recanalization time was 27 minutes. Vessel access: The device successfully reached all intended target vessels across M1, M2, M3, P2, P3, and basilar artery locations. Complication rate: Only 2 intra-procedural complications occurred, neither microcatheter-related. Device reliability: There were no cases requiring usage of an additional or alternative microcatheter.

--- [8] Randomized Controlled Trial of Mechanical Thrombectomy With Anticoagulation Vers (Circulation, 2025) ---
Background: Intermediate-high risk pulmonary embolism patients face elevated risk of clinical deterioration and mortality. The trial tested mechanical thrombectomy combined with anticoagulation against anticoagulation therapy alone. Methods: This international randomized controlled trial enrolled 100 patients across 22 sites with acute PE (symptoms ≤14 days), normotension, RV/LV ratio ≥1.0, and elevated cardiac biomarkers. Participants received either computer-assisted vacuum thrombectomy (CAVT) with anticoagulation (n=47) or anticoagulation alone (n=53). The primary endpoint measured RV/LV ratio changes at 48 hours via blinded imaging assessment. Results: CAVT demonstrated significantly greater RV/LV ratio reduction (0.52±0.37 versus 0.24±0.40; P<0.001). Pulmonary artery obstruction scores improved more substantially with CAVT. Vital sign normalization occurred more frequently within 48 hours following CAVT. Major adverse events within 7 days showed comparable rates between groups: "CAVT, 4.3% versus anticoagulation, 7.5%" (P=0.681). Two PE-related deaths occurred in the CAVT arm. Conclusions: CAVT proved superior to anticoagulation monotherapy for reducing RV/LV ratio within 48 hours, with faster vital sign normalization and comparable safety profiles regarding major adverse events.

--- [9] The SMART Registry: Long-Term Results on the Utility of the Penumbra SMART COIL  (Neurosurgical Focus, 2021) ---
EmboTrap II EXCELLENT registry results: Background: The mTICI 2B score indicates 50-99% reperfusion but represents a broad range. Researchers investigated whether very late leptomeningeal collaterals (VLLC) could further differentiate outcomes in this patient group. Method: A prospective registry of anterior circulation acute ischemic stroke patients treated with EmboTrap devices (2013-2016) was analyzed. Results: Among 177 anterior circulation stroke patients, 94 (53.1%) achieved only mTICI-2B reperfusion. Within this group, 16/94 (17.0%) patients achieved excellent functional outcomes at 3 months and 26 (27.7%) had hyperdensity on follow-up computed tomography. On univariate analysis, VLLC presence was inversely associated with excellent functional outcomes and positively associated with mortality in mTICI-2B patients. Multivariate analysis showed VLLC was inversely associated with excellent outcomes (odds ratio 0.075, 95% CI 0.007-0.765, P = 0.029) but not significantly associated with mortality.

--- [10] Effect of Thrombectomy With Combined Contact Aspiration and Stent Retriever vs S (JAMA, 2021) ---
Background: The study addresses uncertainty about whether combining contact aspiration with stent retriever improves outcomes compared to stent retriever alone for treating acute ischemic stroke caused by large vessel occlusion in the anterior circulation. Methods: This multicenter, randomized controlled trial enrolled 408 patients (405 analyzed) from 11 French stroke centers between October 2017 and May 2018. Participants had anterior circulation large vessel occlusion within 8 hours of symptom onset. Patients were randomly assigned to receive either combined contact aspiration plus stent retriever (205 patients) or stent retriever alone (203 patients). The primary endpoint measured successful reperfusion using the expanded Thrombolysis In Cerebral Infarction score. Results: The combined approach did not significantly improve the primary outcome. Successful near-total/total reperfusion (eTICI 2c/3) occurred in 64.5% of the combined group versus 57.9% of the stent retriever-only group (P = .17). However, the combined technique showed better results for intermediate reperfusion measures after the initial intervention alone (86.2% vs 72.3%, P < .001). Conclusions: Initial thrombectomy using combined contact aspiration and stent retriever did not significantly improve the rate of near-total or total reperfusion compared with stent retriever monotherapy, though the trial may have lacked sufficient power to detect smaller differences.

--- [11] Initial Data Presented for Penumbra's INSIGHT Registry and Subanalysis of the CO (Endovascular Today, 2022) ---
LVIS stent for intracranial aneurysm treatment: The objective was to evaluate the feasibility, efficacy, and safety of the Low-profile Visualized Intraluminal Support (LVIS) device for stent-assisted coiling of wide-necked intracranial aneurysms. Results: Nine studies encompassing 384 patients and 390 aneurysms were analyzed. Technical Success: 96.8% (95% CI 94.4% to 99.1%) achievement rate. Complete Occlusion: 54.6% (95% CI 31.8% to 77.4%) immediately after treatment, improving to 84.3% (95% CI 78.9% to 89.7%) at follow-up. Safety Profile: Procedure-related morbidity was 1.4% (95% CI 0.2% to 2.6%) with zero mortality. Complications: Thromboembolic events occurred in 4.9% (95% CI 1.9% to 7.9%) of cases; hemorrhagic complications were 2.1% (95% CI 0.7% to 3.5%).

--- [12] Technical note and first results on JET 7 thromboaspiration device for T-ICA occ (BMC Neurology, 2022) ---
LVIS EVO stent clinical data: The LVIS EVO is a braided, self-expanding stent approved in Europe (2019) and the US (2024) for treating intracranial aneurysms. Researchers conducted a meta-analysis examining safety outcomes, occlusion rates, and technical success using data from PubMed, MEDLINE, and Embase through August 2024. The study included seven studies evaluating 422 patients with 435 aneurysms. Results: Technical success rate was 99.3% (95% CI, 98.5%-100%). Occlusion Rates: Immediate post-procedure: 75.5% (95% CI, 62.1%-89.0%); At follow-up (mean 256 days): 86.8% (95% CI, 80.4%-93.2%). Complications: Overall rate: 7.4% (95% CI, 2.6%-12.2%); Treatment-related morbidity: 1.8% (95% CI, 0.5%-3.0%); Ischemic-thromboembolic events: 4.7% (95% CI, 1.5%-8.0%); Symptomatic events: 2.2% (95% CI, 0.6%-3.8%).

data.Press Release Title: Penumbra Receives FDA Clearance for RED 62 Reperfusion Catheter
data.Press Release Content:
Penumbra RED 62 Reperfusion Catheter FDA Clearance

Announcement Date: June 23, 2021

Device: RED 62 reperfusion catheter

Regulatory Status: US FDA 510(k) clearance and commercial availability

Indication: Removal of blood clots in acute ischemic stroke patients with large vessel occlusions

Key Features:
- Low diameter profile with extended length for distal vessel access
- Advanced tracking and aspiration technology
- Designed for complex distal vessel anatomy navigation
- Works with the Penumbra ENGINE system

Clinical Quote:
Kurt Reuland, interventional neuroradiologist at CHRISTUS Trinity Mother Frances Health System, stated: The performance and length of RED 62 enable me to address a broader group of patients using aspiration thrombectomy.

Company Perspective:
Adam Elsesser, Penumbra's president and CEO, noted the device builds on our previous design generations while maximizing both trackability and aspiration power to reduce overall healthcare costs.

Significance: RED 62 represents the first catheter in a new RED series, expanding Penumbra's aspiration thrombectomy portfolio for stroke management in challenging distal vessels.

data.Press Release Title: Aspiration Thrombectomy with Penumbra System for Patients with Stroke and Late Onset to Treatment: Subset Analysis of COMPLETE Registry
data.Press Release Content:
Clinical Trial Data Summary: Penumbra System Study

Study Design
This was a retrospective subset analysis from the COMPLETE registry (NCT03464565), a global prospective multicenter study. Researchers examined 167 patients from the full registry of 650 who met specific criteria: anterior circulation large vessel occlusion, adequate imaging scores, and treatment initiated more than 6 hours after stroke onset.

Patient Population
Participants were adults with acute ischemic stroke requiring thrombectomy. The study focused on late onset to treatment (>6 h from stroke onset to puncture), representing a population typically excluded from standard acute intervention protocols.

Primary Findings

Revascularization Success: The procedure achieved successful vessel opening in 83.2% of cases. Notably, ADAPT-only treatment showed superior results at 88.0% compared to combined ADAPT + 3D approach at 75.0%.

Functional Outcomes: Approximately 55.4% of patients achieved good functional independence (modified Rankin Scale 0-2) at 90 days, with mortality at 14.4%.

Safety Profile: No device-related serious adverse events occurred. Procedure-related complications affected 5.4% of patients within 24 hours and 7.2% overall.

Regulatory Status
No specific FDA approval information appears in this document. The study received funding from Penumbra, Inc., which provided database management and statistical support.

data.Article Abstracts & Results:
--- [1] The Penumbra Pivotal Stroke Trial: Safety and Effectiveness of a New Generation  (Stroke, 2009) ---
Penumbra SMART Coil System clinical evaluation: Background: The study aimed to evaluate long-term clinical outcomes for arteriovenous fistula (AVF) patients treated with the Penumbra SMART COIL System through a prospective, multicenter registry analysis. Methods: This subset analysis included AVF patients who underwent endovascular coiling using the SMART COIL system, with monitoring for 12 ± 6 months following treatment. Results: The analysis encompassed 41 patients. Safety: Zero procedural device-related serious adverse events occurred; periprocedural SAE rate was 2.4% (1/41), and no periprocedural deaths occurred. Technical Success: Complete occlusion was achieved in 87.8% (36/41) of patients, with 85.4% (35/41) of the patients avoiding catheter kickout complications. Follow-up Outcomes: At 1 year, the lesion occlusion was better or stable in 93.3% (28/30) of patients. Retreatment: Only 3.4% (1/29) of patients required retreatment during follow-up. Adverse Events: The SAE rate from 24 hours to one year was 26.8%, with 1-year all-cause mortality rate of 2.4% (1/41). Functional Status: At one-year follow-up, 90.9% (20/22) of patients had a modified Rankin Scale score within the range of 0 to 2.

--- [2] Effect of Endovascular Contact Aspiration vs Stent Retriever on Revascularizatio (JAMA, 2017) ---
SOFIA Plus catheter clinical outcomes SESAME/SOFAST registry: No PubMed article found matching this exact search.

--- [3] Aspiration thrombectomy versus stent retriever thrombectomy as first-line approa (The Lancet, 2019) ---
SOFIA catheter aspiration thrombectomy ASTER trial subanalysis: No PubMed article found matching this exact search.

--- [4] Endovascular Treatment of Acute Ischemic Stroke With the Penumbra System in Rout (Stroke, 2021) ---
SOFIA aspiration catheter real-world data: SOFIA 6F Plus Meta-Analysis Study: This research examined direct aspiration using SOFIA 6 F Plus catheters for large-vessel occlusion stroke treatment. The investigation included 144 consecutive patients plus a meta-analysis of nine articles totaling 758 patients. Results: Successful recanalization (mTICI 2b-3) rate of 75.7% was achieved with direct aspiration alone in the cohort. Functional independence at 90 days occurred in 40.3% of cases. Meta-analysis of pooled data revealed: mTICI 2b-3 rate was 71.6% (95%CI, 66.3-76.5%). Rescue stent-retriever backup was needed in 24.1% of cases. Overall success: mTICI 2b-3 rate after DA and rescue therapy was 88.9%. Functional independence: A pooled estimate of 45.6% achieved favorable outcomes. Mortality: 19% within 90 days. Hemorrhage risk: 5.8% (95%CI, 4.2-8.0%) of symptomatic intracranial hemorrhage.

--- [5] Aspiration thrombectomy with the Penumbra System for patients with stroke and la (Frontiers in Neurology, 2023) ---
ERIC retrieval device clinical evaluation: Background: The study evaluated safety and efficacy of mechanical thrombectomy using the ERIC device for acute ischemic stroke patients with distal medium vessel occlusions. Methods: A retrospective analysis examined 50 patients from a prospectively collected database, documenting clinical patient characteristics, procedural measures, timestamps at each stage, and patient outcomes. Results: The cohort included equal gender distribution (50% male/female) with median age 67 years and median NIHSS score of 14. First-pass recanalization outcomes showed: eTICI3 in 32%, eTICI-2c in 16%, eTICI-2B67 in 10%, eTICI2B50 in 6%, and mTICI 0-2A in 36%. The final recanalization rate achieved mTICI 2b-3 in 90% of cases and mTICI 2c-3 in 70%. Conclusion: The ERIC thrombectomy device offers a high rate of first-pass success along with a favorable safety profile.

--- [6] First-Pass Effect With Aspiration Thrombectomy With the Penumbra System in Patie (Stroke: Vascular and Interventional Neurology, 2022) ---
ERIC retrieval device multicenter study: No additional specific multicenter ERIC study found beyond Row 70.

--- [7] Penumbra, Inc. Announces Completion of THUNDER IDE Study Enrollment for Acute Is (Press Release, 2024) ---
Headway microcatheter clinical performance: The Headway27 microcatheter features optimal dimensions: 156 cm length, 2.6 Fr distal OD, ID 0.027 inches with excellent visibility for stroke procedures. Results: The study demonstrated strong clinical performance in 50 consecutive thrombectomy cases. Thrombectomy approaches: Manual aspiration alone occurred in 72% of cases, with stentriever-assisted techniques in 6% and combined methods in 22%. Recanalization success: mTICI 2B/3 recanalization was achieved in 94% of cases. Time efficiency: Median groin puncture to final recanalization time was 27 minutes. Vessel access: The device successfully reached all intended target vessels across M1, M2, M3, P2, P3, and basilar artery locations. Complication rate: Only 2 intra-procedural complications occurred, neither microcatheter-related. Device reliability: There were no cases requiring usage of an additional or alternative microcatheter.

--- [8] Randomized Controlled Trial of Mechanical Thrombectomy With Anticoagulation Vers (Circulation, 2025) ---
Background: Intermediate-high risk pulmonary embolism patients face elevated risk of clinical deterioration and mortality. The trial tested mechanical thrombectomy combined with anticoagulation against anticoagulation therapy alone. Methods: This international randomized controlled trial enrolled 100 patients across 22 sites with acute PE (symptoms ≤14 days), normotension, RV/LV ratio ≥1.0, and elevated cardiac biomarkers. Participants received either computer-assisted vacuum thrombectomy (CAVT) with anticoagulation (n=47) or anticoagulation alone (n=53). The primary endpoint measured RV/LV ratio changes at 48 hours via blinded imaging assessment. Results: CAVT demonstrated significantly greater RV/LV ratio reduction (0.52±0.37 versus 0.24±0.40; P<0.001). Pulmonary artery obstruction scores improved more substantially with CAVT. Vital sign normalization occurred more frequently within 48 hours following CAVT. Major adverse events within 7 days showed comparable rates between groups: "CAVT, 4.3% versus anticoagulation, 7.5%" (P=0.681). Two PE-related deaths occurred in the CAVT arm. Conclusions: CAVT proved superior to anticoagulation monotherapy for reducing RV/LV ratio within 48 hours, with faster vital sign normalization and comparable safety profiles regarding major adverse events.

--- [9] The SMART Registry: Long-Term Results on the Utility of the Penumbra SMART COIL  (Neurosurgical Focus, 2021) ---
EmboTrap II EXCELLENT registry results: Background: The mTICI 2B score indicates 50-99% reperfusion but represents a broad range. Researchers investigated whether very late leptomeningeal collaterals (VLLC) could further differentiate outcomes in this patient group. Method: A prospective registry of anterior circulation acute ischemic stroke patients treated with EmboTrap devices (2013-2016) was analyzed. Results: Among 177 anterior circulation stroke patients, 94 (53.1%) achieved only mTICI-2B reperfusion. Within this group, 16/94 (17.0%) patients achieved excellent functional outcomes at 3 months and 26 (27.7%) had hyperdensity on follow-up computed tomography. On univariate analysis, VLLC presence was inversely associated with excellent functional outcomes and positively associated with mortality in mTICI-2B patients. Multivariate analysis showed VLLC was inversely associated with excellent outcomes (odds ratio 0.075, 95% CI 0.007-0.765, P = 0.029) but not significantly associated with mortality.

--- [10] Effect of Thrombectomy With Combined Contact Aspiration and Stent Retriever vs S (JAMA, 2021) ---
Background: The study addresses uncertainty about whether combining contact aspiration with stent retriever improves outcomes compared to stent retriever alone for treating acute ischemic stroke caused by large vessel occlusion in the anterior circulation. Methods: This multicenter, randomized controlled trial enrolled 408 patients (405 analyzed) from 11 French stroke centers between October 2017 and May 2018. Participants had anterior circulation large vessel occlusion within 8 hours of symptom onset. Patients were randomly assigned to receive either combined contact aspiration plus stent retriever (205 patients) or stent retriever alone (203 patients). The primary endpoint measured successful reperfusion using the expanded Thrombolysis In Cerebral Infarction score. Results: The combined approach did not significantly improve the primary outcome. Successful near-total/total reperfusion (eTICI 2c/3) occurred in 64.5% of the combined group versus 57.9% of the stent retriever-only group (P = .17). However, the combined technique showed better results for intermediate reperfusion measures after the initial intervention alone (86.2% vs 72.3%, P < .001). Conclusions: Initial thrombectomy using combined contact aspiration and stent retriever did not significantly improve the rate of near-total or total reperfusion compared with stent retriever monotherapy, though the trial may have lacked sufficient power to detect smaller differences.

--- [11] Initial Data Presented for Penumbra's INSIGHT Registry and Subanalysis of the CO (Endovascular Today, 2022) ---
LVIS stent for intracranial aneurysm treatment: The objective was to evaluate the feasibility, efficacy, and safety of the Low-profile Visualized Intraluminal Support (LVIS) device for stent-assisted coiling of wide-necked intracranial aneurysms. Results: Nine studies encompassing 384 patients and 390 aneurysms were analyzed. Technical Success: 96.8% (95% CI 94.4% to 99.1%) achievement rate. Complete Occlusion: 54.6% (95% CI 31.8% to 77.4%) immediately after treatment, improving to 84.3% (95% CI 78.9% to 89.7%) at follow-up. Safety Profile: Procedure-related morbidity was 1.4% (95% CI 0.2% to 2.6%) with zero mortality. Complications: Thromboembolic events occurred in 4.9% (95% CI 1.9% to 7.9%) of cases; hemorrhagic complications were 2.1% (95% CI 0.7% to 3.5%).

--- [12] Technical note and first results on JET 7 thromboaspiration device for T-ICA occ (BMC Neurology, 2022) ---
LVIS EVO stent clinical data: The LVIS EVO is a braided, self-expanding stent approved in Europe (2019) and the US (2024) for treating intracranial aneurysms. Researchers conducted a meta-analysis examining safety outcomes, occlusion rates, and technical success using data from PubMed, MEDLINE, and Embase through August 2024. The study included seven studies evaluating 422 patients with 435 aneurysms. Results: Technical success rate was 99.3% (95% CI, 98.5%-100%). Occlusion Rates: Immediate post-procedure: 75.5% (95% CI, 62.1%-89.0%); At follow-up (mean 256 days): 86.8% (95% CI, 80.4%-93.2%). Complications: Overall rate: 7.4% (95% CI, 2.6%-12.2%); Treatment-related morbidity: 1.8% (95% CI, 0.5%-3.0%); Ischemic-thromboembolic events: 4.7% (95% CI, 1.5%-8.0%); Symptomatic events: 2.2% (95% CI, 0.6%-3.8%).

data.Press Release Title: First-Pass Effect with Aspiration Thrombectomy Using Penumbra System: Core Lab-Adjudicated Subset of COMPLETE Registry. Published in Stroke: VIN.
data.Press Release Content: First-Pass Effect With Aspiration Thrombectomy With the Penumbra System in Patients With Acute Ischemic Stroke: A Core Laboratory–Adjudicated Subset Analysis of the COMPLETE Registry. The COMPLETE (International Acute Ischemic Stroke Registry With the Penumbra System Aspiration Including the 3D Revascularization Device) registry was a global, prospective, postmarket, multicenter registry evaluating the generalizability of the safety and efficacy of the Penumbra System in real-world settings. Six hundred fifty patients were enrolled (median age 70 years, 54.0% female, 49.2% given intravenous recombinant tissue-type plasminogen activator before thrombectomy). The rate of modified Thrombolysis in Cerebral Infarction 2b to 3 postprocedure was 87.8%. First pass and postprocedure rates of modified Thrombolysis in Cerebral Infarction 2c to 3 were 41.5% and 66.2%, respectively. At 90 days, 55.8% had modified Rankin Scale score 0 to 2, and all-cause mortality was 15.5%. First-pass effect (FPE), defined as successful or complete revascularization with one pass of a mechanical thrombectomy device, reduces time to revascularization. For aspiration thrombectomy with the Penumbra System, achieving FPE was associated with a significantly higher rate of good functional outcome.

data.Press Release Title: Penumbra Launches New RED 43 Reperfusion Catheter for Mechanical Thrombectomy
data.Press Release Content:
Penumbra RED 43 Reperfusion Catheter Launch

Announcement
Penumbra announced the launch of the RED 43 reperfusion catheter, described as taking distal aspiration to the next level with optimized lumen occupation and soft distal design for enhanced delivery in tortuous vessels.

Device Specifications
- Lumen size: 0.043 inches
- Outer dimension: 1.52mm (0.060 inches)
- Key features: Low-profile, atraumatic design with articulating marker band and short polymer tip
- Coating technology: REDglide hydrophilic coating for smooth trackability
- Liner: Full-length polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE) for durability under aspiration
- Coil construction: Proximal hybrid stainless steel and nitinol for optimal support

Clinical Data
No clinical trial data was included in the announcement.

Regulatory Status
The device received FDA 510(k) premarket clearance on December 20, 2022 (notification ID: K222808).

Intended Use
Designed for mechanical thrombectomy procedures, compatible with the Penumbra ENGINE aspiration system.

Dates
- Launch date: April 27, 2023
- FDA approval: December 20, 2022

data.Article Abstracts & Results:
--- [1] The Penumbra Pivotal Stroke Trial: Safety and Effectiveness of a New Generation  (Stroke, 2009) ---
Penumbra SMART Coil System clinical evaluation: Background: The study aimed to evaluate long-term clinical outcomes for arteriovenous fistula (AVF) patients treated with the Penumbra SMART COIL System through a prospective, multicenter registry analysis. Methods: This subset analysis included AVF patients who underwent endovascular coiling using the SMART COIL system, with monitoring for 12 ± 6 months following treatment. Results: The analysis encompassed 41 patients. Safety: Zero procedural device-related serious adverse events occurred; periprocedural SAE rate was 2.4% (1/41), and no periprocedural deaths occurred. Technical Success: Complete occlusion was achieved in 87.8% (36/41) of patients, with 85.4% (35/41) of the patients avoiding catheter kickout complications. Follow-up Outcomes: At 1 year, the lesion occlusion was better or stable in 93.3% (28/30) of patients. Retreatment: Only 3.4% (1/29) of patients required retreatment during follow-up. Adverse Events: The SAE rate from 24 hours to one year was 26.8%, with 1-year all-cause mortality rate of 2.4% (1/41). Functional Status: At one-year follow-up, 90.9% (20/22) of patients had a modified Rankin Scale score within the range of 0 to 2.

--- [2] Effect of Endovascular Contact Aspiration vs Stent Retriever on Revascularizatio (JAMA, 2017) ---
SOFIA Plus catheter clinical outcomes SESAME/SOFAST registry: No PubMed article found matching this exact search.

--- [3] Aspiration thrombectomy versus stent retriever thrombectomy as first-line approa (The Lancet, 2019) ---
SOFIA catheter aspiration thrombectomy ASTER trial subanalysis: No PubMed article found matching this exact search.

--- [4] Endovascular Treatment of Acute Ischemic Stroke With the Penumbra System in Rout (Stroke, 2021) ---
SOFIA aspiration catheter real-world data: SOFIA 6F Plus Meta-Analysis Study: This research examined direct aspiration using SOFIA 6 F Plus catheters for large-vessel occlusion stroke treatment. The investigation included 144 consecutive patients plus a meta-analysis of nine articles totaling 758 patients. Results: Successful recanalization (mTICI 2b-3) rate of 75.7% was achieved with direct aspiration alone in the cohort. Functional independence at 90 days occurred in 40.3% of cases. Meta-analysis of pooled data revealed: mTICI 2b-3 rate was 71.6% (95%CI, 66.3-76.5%). Rescue stent-retriever backup was needed in 24.1% of cases. Overall success: mTICI 2b-3 rate after DA and rescue therapy was 88.9%. Functional independence: A pooled estimate of 45.6% achieved favorable outcomes. Mortality: 19% within 90 days. Hemorrhage risk: 5.8% (95%CI, 4.2-8.0%) of symptomatic intracranial hemorrhage.

--- [5] Aspiration thrombectomy with the Penumbra System for patients with stroke and la (Frontiers in Neurology, 2023) ---
ERIC retrieval device clinical evaluation: Background: The study evaluated safety and efficacy of mechanical thrombectomy using the ERIC device for acute ischemic stroke patients with distal medium vessel occlusions. Methods: A retrospective analysis examined 50 patients from a prospectively collected database, documenting clinical patient characteristics, procedural measures, timestamps at each stage, and patient outcomes. Results: The cohort included equal gender distribution (50% male/female) with median age 67 years and median NIHSS score of 14. First-pass recanalization outcomes showed: eTICI3 in 32%, eTICI-2c in 16%, eTICI-2B67 in 10%, eTICI2B50 in 6%, and mTICI 0-2A in 36%. The final recanalization rate achieved mTICI 2b-3 in 90% of cases and mTICI 2c-3 in 70%. Conclusion: The ERIC thrombectomy device offers a high rate of first-pass success along with a favorable safety profile.

--- [6] First-Pass Effect With Aspiration Thrombectomy With the Penumbra System in Patie (Stroke: Vascular and Interventional Neurology, 2022) ---
ERIC retrieval device multicenter study: No additional specific multicenter ERIC study found beyond Row 70.

--- [7] Initial Data Presented for Penumbra's INSIGHT Registry and Subanalysis of the CO (Endovascular Today, 2022) ---
LVIS stent for intracranial aneurysm treatment: The objective was to evaluate the feasibility, efficacy, and safety of the Low-profile Visualized Intraluminal Support (LVIS) device for stent-assisted coiling of wide-necked intracranial aneurysms. Results: Nine studies encompassing 384 patients and 390 aneurysms were analyzed. Technical Success: 96.8% (95% CI 94.4% to 99.1%) achievement rate. Complete Occlusion: 54.6% (95% CI 31.8% to 77.4%) immediately after treatment, improving to 84.3% (95% CI 78.9% to 89.7%) at follow-up. Safety Profile: Procedure-related morbidity was 1.4% (95% CI 0.2% to 2.6%) with zero mortality. Complications: Thromboembolic events occurred in 4.9% (95% CI 1.9% to 7.9%) of cases; hemorrhagic complications were 2.1% (95% CI 0.7% to 3.5%).

--- [8] Penumbra, Inc. Announces Completion of THUNDER IDE Study Enrollment for Acute Is (Press Release, 2024) ---
Headway microcatheter clinical performance: The Headway27 microcatheter features optimal dimensions: 156 cm length, 2.6 Fr distal OD, ID 0.027 inches with excellent visibility for stroke procedures. Results: The study demonstrated strong clinical performance in 50 consecutive thrombectomy cases. Thrombectomy approaches: Manual aspiration alone occurred in 72% of cases, with stentriever-assisted techniques in 6% and combined methods in 22%. Recanalization success: mTICI 2B/3 recanalization was achieved in 94% of cases. Time efficiency: Median groin puncture to final recanalization time was 27 minutes. Vessel access: The device successfully reached all intended target vessels across M1, M2, M3, P2, P3, and basilar artery locations. Complication rate: Only 2 intra-procedural complications occurred, neither microcatheter-related. Device reliability: There were no cases requiring usage of an additional or alternative microcatheter.

--- [9] Randomized Controlled Trial of Mechanical Thrombectomy With Anticoagulation Vers (Circulation, 2025) ---
Background: Intermediate-high risk pulmonary embolism patients face elevated risk of clinical deterioration and mortality. The trial tested mechanical thrombectomy combined with anticoagulation against anticoagulation therapy alone. Methods: This international randomized controlled trial enrolled 100 patients across 22 sites with acute PE (symptoms ≤14 days), normotension, RV/LV ratio ≥1.0, and elevated cardiac biomarkers. Participants received either computer-assisted vacuum thrombectomy (CAVT) with anticoagulation (n=47) or anticoagulation alone (n=53). The primary endpoint measured RV/LV ratio changes at 48 hours via blinded imaging assessment. Results: CAVT demonstrated significantly greater RV/LV ratio reduction (0.52±0.37 versus 0.24±0.40; P<0.001). Pulmonary artery obstruction scores improved more substantially with CAVT. Vital sign normalization occurred more frequently within 48 hours following CAVT. Major adverse events within 7 days showed comparable rates between groups: "CAVT, 4.3% versus anticoagulation, 7.5%" (P=0.681). Two PE-related deaths occurred in the CAVT arm. Conclusions: CAVT proved superior to anticoagulation monotherapy for reducing RV/LV ratio within 48 hours, with faster vital sign normalization and comparable safety profiles regarding major adverse events.

--- [10] The SMART Registry: Long-Term Results on the Utility of the Penumbra SMART COIL  (Neurosurgical Focus, 2021) ---
EmboTrap II EXCELLENT registry results: Background: The mTICI 2B score indicates 50-99% reperfusion but represents a broad range. Researchers investigated whether very late leptomeningeal collaterals (VLLC) could further differentiate outcomes in this patient group. Method: A prospective registry of anterior circulation acute ischemic stroke patients treated with EmboTrap devices (2013-2016) was analyzed. Results: Among 177 anterior circulation stroke patients, 94 (53.1%) achieved only mTICI-2B reperfusion. Within this group, 16/94 (17.0%) patients achieved excellent functional outcomes at 3 months and 26 (27.7%) had hyperdensity on follow-up computed tomography. On univariate analysis, VLLC presence was inversely associated with excellent functional outcomes and positively associated with mortality in mTICI-2B patients. Multivariate analysis showed VLLC was inversely associated with excellent outcomes (odds ratio 0.075, 95% CI 0.007-0.765, P = 0.029) but not significantly associated with mortality.

--- [11] Effect of Thrombectomy With Combined Contact Aspiration and Stent Retriever vs S (JAMA, 2021) ---
Background: The study addresses uncertainty about whether combining contact aspiration with stent retriever improves outcomes compared to stent retriever alone for treating acute ischemic stroke caused by large vessel occlusion in the anterior circulation. Methods: This multicenter, randomized controlled trial enrolled 408 patients (405 analyzed) from 11 French stroke centers between October 2017 and May 2018. Participants had anterior circulation large vessel occlusion within 8 hours of symptom onset. Patients were randomly assigned to receive either combined contact aspiration plus stent retriever (205 patients) or stent retriever alone (203 patients). The primary endpoint measured successful reperfusion using the expanded Thrombolysis In Cerebral Infarction score. Results: The combined approach did not significantly improve the primary outcome. Successful near-total/total reperfusion (eTICI 2c/3) occurred in 64.5% of the combined group versus 57.9% of the stent retriever-only group (P = .17). However, the combined technique showed better results for intermediate reperfusion measures after the initial intervention alone (86.2% vs 72.3%, P < .001). Conclusions: Initial thrombectomy using combined contact aspiration and stent retriever did not significantly improve the rate of near-total or total reperfusion compared with stent retriever monotherapy, though the trial may have lacked sufficient power to detect smaller differences.

data.Press Release Title: Penumbra Expands Computer-Aided Thrombectomy Offerings with Lightning Bolt 7 — Modulated Aspiration Technology
data.Press Release Content: Penumbra Launches Lightning Bolt 7 Thrombectomy System. Penumbra received FDA clearance for Lightning Bolt 7, an advanced arterial thrombectomy system designed to remove blood clots using modulated aspiration technique. The device combines intelligent aspiration technology with a microprocessor algorithm to rapidly extract large, fibrous blood clots from arteries while minimizing blood loss. It targets conditions including acute limb ischemia, hibernating thrombus, and visceral occlusions. Powered by the Penumbra ENGINE, the system detects differences between clot and blood flow and identifies friction within the catheter to orchestrate rapid valve opening/closing to disrupt resistance. Compatible with catheters featuring MaxID hypotube technology for larger inner diameter with smaller outer profile. Received FDA clearance in March 2023. Earlier Indigo System data showed 98% limb salvage rates at 30 days, average procedure time of 22 minutes, and 3% 30-day mortality. According to physicians, computer-aided technology offers the promise of unheralded procedural efficiency.

data.Press Release Title: Penumbra Launches Midway Intermediate Catheters and Expands European Stroke Portfolio
data.Press Release Content:
Penumbra Launches Midway Intermediate Catheters and Expands European Stroke Portfolio

Announcement Details
Penumbra announced the launch of Midway 43 and Midway 62 intermediate delivery catheters alongside CE-mark certification for three reperfusion catheters (Red 43, Red 72 with Sendit technology, and Red 78) for European markets on May 7, 2024.

Device Features

Midway Catheters:
- Designed for precise delivery of microcatheters, embolisation coils, intrasaccular devices, and flow diverters
- Compatible with Benchmark 71, BMX 81, and BMX 96 access systems
- Features advanced catheter design with full-length PTFE liner and distal hydrophilic coating
- Incorporates Red catheter technology for optimal support and trackability balance

Red Reperfusion Catheters:
- Engineered with Redglide technology for enhanced trackability in cerebral vasculature
- Full-length PTFE liner maintains true lumen size under vacuum
- Optimised for science-based aspiration thrombectomy (S-BAT)

Clinical Perspective
John Chaloupka (Mount Sinai Medical Center) noted the field faces increasingly demanding scenarios requiring intermediate access guiding catheters for challenging microcatheterisation and tortuous vasculature navigation.

Regulatory Status
Both product lines received CE-mark certification, enabling European market distribution.

data.Device/Product: Lightning Bolt 12 + Lightning Bolt 6X with TraX
data.Press Release Title: Announcing Lightning Bolt 12 and Lightning Bolt 6X with TraX — Newest Additions to CAVT Platform
data.Press Release Content:
Penumbra Lightning Bolt Announcement Summary

Key Announcement
Penumbra launched Lightning Bolt 12 and Lightning Bolt 6X with TraX—next-generation computer-assisted vacuum thrombectomy (CAVT) systems for arterial and venous thrombus management.

Publication Date: January 22, 2025

Device Details

Lightning Bolt 12:
- First thrombectomy system designed for venous thromboembolism with modulated aspiration
- Three times faster than Lightning 12 with Separator 12
- Uses Bolt 2.0 algorithm with escalated modulation for high thrombus burden
- Applications: upper extremity, portal veins, large arterial thrombus

Lightning Bolt 6X with TraX:
- Enables access to smaller vessels while maintaining 6F vascular access
- Features advanced coil-wind technology for precise control
- Includes enhanced hydrophilic coating to reduce friction
- Packaged with TraX Dilator offering 99% ledge reduction

Clinical Data
Performance based on bench testing comparing aspiration times. Per disclaimer: Bench test results may not be indicative of clinical performance.

Key Quote
Dr. James F. Benenati, CMO: These new devices are designed to significantly improve procedure time, accelerate aspiration, and reach smaller vessels.

Regulatory
Devices are restricted to sale by physician order. Full indications, contraindications, and warnings available in Instructions for Use.

data.Press Release Title: Penumbra Launches Lightning Flash Mechanical Thrombectomy System After FDA Clearance
data.Press Release Content:
Penumbra Lightning Flash Mechanical Thrombectomy System

Announcement Date: January 10, 2023

Regulatory Status: FDA clearance received and product launched

Device Overview:
The Lightning Flash is a mechanical thrombectomy system designed to remove large blood clots from the body, including venous thrombus and pulmonary emboli. The device features dual clot detection algorithms and is powered by the Penumbra Engine.

Key Technical Features:
- Lightning intelligent aspiration technology with dual clot detection algorithms
- Pressure and flow-based algorithms for detecting clot and blood flow
- MaxID hypertube technology enabling large inner diameter while maintaining lower profile
- Soft, atraumatic tip for navigating complex anatomy

Clinical Data:
The Lightning Flash is part of Penumbra's Indigo system portfolio. Early data from the STRIKE-PE study showed improvement in clinical outcomes and quality of life, presented by Dr. Ido Weinberg at the VIVA22 conference.

Market Position:
This system represents an expansion of Penumbra's existing Lightning computer-aided mechanical thrombectomy platform in the venous intervention space.

data.Press Release Title: Penumbra Says Stroke Device Delayed at Least 12 Months Due to FDA Request for Additional Study
data.Press Release Content:
Penumbra Stroke Device Delayed by FDA Request

Key Announcement:
Penumbra has announced that its stroke treatment device will experience a significant delay of at least 12 months due to an FDA information request.

Device Details:
The announcement concerns a stroke device currently under FDA review. While the full technical specifications aren't detailed in the provided content, the device relates to Penumbra's brain clot treatment technology.

FDA Delay:
The company attributes the postponement to a regulatory request from the FDA, pushing the expected timeline back by a minimum of one year from the announcement date in August 2023.

Available Quote:
The article references this situation but the specific direct quotes from company officials or the FDA are not clearly isolated in the provided page content. The headline itself states: Penumbra says stroke device delayed at least 12 months due to FDA request

Context:
This brief was published on August 4, 2023, by Nick Paul Taylor for MedTech Dive and touches on themes relevant to medical device development, FDA oversight, and clinical trial timelines.

data.Press Release Title: Penumbra Announces RED 72 Silver Label with SENDit — 50% Increase in Trackability
data.Press Release Content:
Penumbra Red 72 Silver Label Announcement Summary

Device Overview
Penumbra announced an updated version of its Red 72 large-bore reperfusion catheter, now called the Red 72 Silver Label. The device features refined transition zones designed to better accommodate distal brain anatomy.

Key Enhancements
- 50% increase in trackability compared to the original Red 72 with SENDit technology
- Incorporates engineering advancements previously used in Red 43 and Red 62 catheters
- SENDit configuration aims to minimize ledge effect and simplify preparation

Clinical Design
The Red 72 is engineered to occupy 72-77% of the average M1 segment of the middle cerebral artery, maintaining optimal catheter-to-vessel diameter ratios needed for effective thrombectomy. This aligns with Penumbra's Science-Based Aspiration Thrombectomy (S-BAT) principles.

Clinical Feedback
Daniel Vela Duarte (Palm Beach Neuroscience Institute): The change and overall performance are radically better.

Ian Kaminsky (RIA Neurovascular) reported successful navigation to an M1 occlusion in a nonagenarian patient with severe cervical tortuosity.

Announcement Date
April 24, 2025

data.Press Release Title: Penumbra Launches Lightning Flash 2.0 — Latest CAVT Technology Designed to Rapidly Remove Blood Clots
data.Press Release Content:
Penumbra Lightning Flash 2.0 Announcement Summary

Device & Announcement
Product: Lightning Flash 2.0 - next-generation computer-assisted vacuum thrombectomy (CAVT) system

Announcement Date: April 23, 2024

Status: FDA-cleared and launched for U.S. market

Key Features
- Advanced algorithms designed for increased speed and sensitivity to thrombus and blood flow
- Streamlined audio-visual feedback for enhanced physician guidance
- Novel catheter technology for improved navigation and clot removal with minimal blood loss

Clinical Benefits
According to Dr. James F. Benenati, Chief Medical Officer: Lightning Flash 2.0 has significantly improved procedure time by shortening the aspiration time. It has also shown reductions in blood removed during aspiration.

The device aims to improve patient safety, enhance clinical outcomes, and streamline physician efficiency.

Indications
- Removal of venous thrombus
- Treatment of pulmonary emboli (PE)

Company Context
Penumbra, Inc. (NYSE: PEN) is described as the world's largest thrombectomy company. Lightning Flash 2.0 is part of the Indigo System with Lightning portfolio—currently the only computer-assisted mechanical thrombectomy systems available in the U.S.

Additional Note
Early clinical data has demonstrated improvements in patient clinical outcomes and quality of life.

data.Press Release Title: Penumbra Inc. Announces FDA 510(k) Clearance of the Penumbra System for Revascularization of Patients with Acute Ischemic Stroke — First Aspiration-Based Thrombectomy System
data.Press Release Content:
Penumbra System 510(k) Clearance Announcement

Key Announcement
Penumbra, Inc. received FDA 510(k) clearance for the Penumbra System, a device designed to remove blood clots from brain vessels in acute ischemic stroke patients within 8 hours of symptom onset.

Device Details
The system consists of aspiration devices sized to match specific brain vessel anatomy. It works on the clot's surface to safely remove occlusions without requiring navigation beyond the blockage. A clot-grabbing device was included in trials but excluded from the final submission due to limited necessity.

Clinical Data
A 125-patient pivotal trial demonstrated the device's performance and efficacy. Results were scheduled for presentation at the 2008 ASA International Stroke Conference in New Orleans on February 22, 2008, in the Late Breaking Science category.

FDA & Regulatory Status
- 510(k) Clearance: January 2008
- Location: San Leandro, California
- CE Mark: Already obtained; device commercially available in Europe since June 2007
- U.S. Commercialization: Immediate

Key Quote
Dr. Michael Madison stated: This system of tools is an important advance that may transform the acute treatment of this devastating disease.

Clinical Significance
The device addresses a critical gap for stroke patients presenting beyond the three-hour window for intravenous thrombolysis, offering potential revascularization options.
